Fleet"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"","venue":"ICLR","year":2024,"track":"main","award":"poster","links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/iclr/ClarkVSF24","doi":""},"_dblp_key":"conf/iclr/ClarkVSF24","_ee":"https://openreview.net/forum?id=1vmSEVL19f","category_id":"post_train","relevance_score":6,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇工作要解决的是：扩散模型想用“奖励”做对齐或偏好优化时，常见做法是采样后用不可微指标打分再做近似更新，信号噪声大且样本效率低。过去缺少一个能直接对扩散采样过程反传奖励的稳定方案。","importance":"贡献是扩散模型的对齐/后训练方法级：直接在可微奖励上微调扩散模型，把 RLHF 类思想更干净地迁移到 diffusion。对 pretrain 的启示是：扩散生成的训练-采样闭环可以像 policy 一样被 reward shaping，但稳定性与 reward 设计会成为主要瓶颈；代价是需要可微 reward 或可微代理模型，覆盖面受限。","technical_approach":"方法核心是把扩散采样写成可微计算图，对最终样本的 reward 通过采样轨迹反传到模型参数，从而进行直接 fine-tuning。关键设计通常包括：用重参数化/可微近似处理噪声注入，控制梯度方差（例如截断、baseline、分步加权），并避免把模型推向 reward hacking 的窄模式。与基于 REINFORCE 的黑盒优化相比，可微路径梯度更高效，但要求 reward 对像素/潜变量可微且数值稳定。","experiments":"未提供摘要与实验细节，无法给出使用的扩散架构（U-Net/DiT/latent diffusion）、reward 类型（美学、对齐、可控属性）、以及相对 DDPO/DPPOK 等方法的提升数字；建议重点核对：梯度方差与训练稳定性曲线、在相同采样预算下的样本效率、以及是否出现模式坍缩或对 reward 过拟合。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper tackles diffusion alignment with rewards: common approaches score samples with non-differentiable metrics and use approximate updates, which are noisy and sample-inefficient. A stable way to backpropagate reward through the diffusion sampling process has been missing.","importance":"This is a post-training/alignment method for diffusion models: directly fine-tuning on differentiable rewards, bringing RLHF-like optimization more cleanly to diffusion. For pretraining, it highlights that diffusion generation can be treated like a policy optimized by reward shaping, but stability and reward design become the main bottlenecks. The limitation is reliance on differentiable rewards or differentiable surrogates.","technical_approach":"The core idea is to make the diffusion sampling trajectory a differentiable computation graph and backpropagate the final-sample reward through the trajectory into model parameters. Key engineering typically includes reparameterization for noise injection, variance control (baselines/truncation/step weighting), and safeguards against reward hacking and mode collapse. Compared to REINFORCE-style black-box updates, pathwise gradients are more efficient but require numerically stable differentiable rewards.","experiments":"No abstract/experimental details were provided, so architectures (U-Net/DiT/latent diffusion), reward types (aesthetics/alignment/attributes), and gains vs DDPO-like baselines cannot be stated. Fleet"]},{"id":"CundyE24","title":"SequenceMatch: Imitation Learning for Autoregressive Sequence Modelling with Backtracking","authors":["Chris Cundy","Stefano Ermon"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"","venue":"ICLR","year":2024,"track":"main","award":"poster","links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/iclr/CundyE24","doi":""},"_dblp_key":"conf/iclr/CundyE24","_ee":"https://openreview.net/forum?id=FJWT0692hw","category_id":"post_train","relevance_score":6,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇工作要解决的是：自回归序列建模在训练时用 teacher forcing，推理时却要吃自己的输出，导致暴露偏差（exposure bias）与错误累积。过去用 scheduled sampling 或序列级 RL 缓解，但要么不稳定，要么样本效率差。","importance":"贡献是训练目标/学习算法：SequenceMatch 用“带回溯的模仿学习”把训练分布更贴近推理分布，同时保持比纯 RL 更稳定的优化。对 pretrain 的启示是：即使在大模型时代，训练-推理分布不一致仍会影响长序列质量；代价是训练过程更复杂，且回溯策略设计会影响收敛与计算量。","technical_approach":"核心做法是把生成过程视为一个决策过程：模型按当前策略生成 token，但允许在发现偏离参考序列/高损失片段时回溯（backtracking）并重新对齐到更合理的前缀，然后用模仿学习信号更新。这样能在训练中覆盖“模型自己会走到的状态”，减少只在金标准前缀上学习带来的偏差。与 scheduled sampling 的随机替换不同，回溯是更有针对性的状态分布修正；边界在于回溯会增加计算，并且对参考序列质量与对齐准则敏感。","experiments":"未提供摘要与实验细节，无法给出任务（语言建模/程序生成等）、模型规模与具体提升；建议重点核对：与 teacher forcing、scheduled sampling、序列级 RL 的对比，尤其是长序列错误率/困惑度与生成一致性指标；以及回溯频率对 compute 与收益的 scaling 关系。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper targets exposure bias in autoregressive modeling: training with teacher forcing but generating from the model’s own outputs at inference leads to compounding errors. Prior fixes like scheduled sampling or sequence-level RL can be unstable or sample-inefficient.","importance":"This is a training-objective / learning-algorithm contribution: SequenceMatch uses imitation learning with backtracking to better match the training state distribution to the inference distribution while staying more stable than pure RL. For pretraining, it underscores that train–test mismatch still matters for long-horizon generation; the trade-off is added training complexity and compute from backtracking.","technical_approach":"The method treats generation as a decision process: the model generates under its current policy, but when it deviates from the reference or enters high-loss regions, it backtracks to a better-aligned prefix and continues, then updates via imitation signals. This exposes the model to states it actually visits, reducing the bias of learning only on gold prefixes. Compared to scheduled sampling’s random token replacement, backtracking is targeted distribution correction. Limitations include extra compute and sensitivity to reference quality and alignment criteria.","experiments":"No abstract/experimental details were provided, so tasks, model sizes, and gains cannot be stated. Pure RL struggles under sparse rewards and long action chains, while pure language planning often stays too abstract to become executable control. The goal is to combine high-level priors from an LM with low-level closed-loop learning from RL.","importance":"This is mainly a method and systems contribution rather than a new pretraining paradigm. The useful lesson for foundation-model work is that language models can do more than supervised policy generation: they can provide structured exploration bias and subgoal proposals that materially help long-horizon decision making. That is a concrete interface from pretraining to embodied control.","technical_approach":"The core idea is a plan-then-learn pipeline: an LM proposes a decomposition or action sequence sketch, and RL learns execution within that scaffold. This differs from simply feeding language as another observation. The explicit hierarchy matters because it breaks a long horizon into shorter local problems, which reduces exploration burden. Registers","authors":["Timothée Darcet","Maxime Oquab","Julien Mairal","Piotr Bojanowski"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"","venue":"ICLR","year":2024,"track":"main","award":"oral","links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/iclr/DarcetOMB24","doi":""},"_dblp_key":"conf/iclr/DarcetOMB24","_ee":"https://openreview.net/forum?id=2dnO3LLiJ1","category_id":"mechanism","relevance_score":6,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇论文的结论很明确：很多 ViT 会在中间层自发长出少量“高范数异常 token”，它们不像正常 patch token 那样承载局部视觉信息，反而像临时工作内存，导致特征图出现伪影并伤害密集预测。过去大家往往把这些现象归因于训练噪声或架构细节，但没有直接给模型一个专门存放全局计算中间量的位置。","importance":"它的重要性在于提出了一个极小但很稳的架构修补：给 ViT 加 register tokens。对 pretrain 和 VLM 预训练的启示很强，因为这不是下游头部小技巧，而是 backbone 表示结构的问题；如果视觉主干在预训练时就把 patch token 当“缓存槽”使用，那么后续检索、分割、定位甚至多模态对齐都会受影响。","technical_approach":"方法很简单也很有针对性：在输入序列里额外加入少量可学习 register token，让模型把需要全局汇总或中间计算的内容放到这些专用槽位，而不是污染 patch token。它不是再加一个 cls token 的重复版本，因为 cls token 通常承担读出语义，而 registers 更像内部 scratchpad；和增加模型宽度或层数相比，这种改动几乎不碰主干计算图，却直接改变了 token 间的功能分工。之所以有效，是因为 self-attention 会自然利用这些专门 token 做全局路由；边界在于它主要缓解表示污染问题，不会替代更强数据或更大模型。","experiments":"论文最有价值的实验事实通常包括两部分。第一，作者可视化并量化了异常高范数 token 的存在，显示它们会在 DINOv2 等 ViT 中出现，并与特征图伪影相关。第二，加入少量 registers 后，图像表示更平滑，密集任务如分割或目标发现类指标明显改善，同时图像级分类不会受损，甚至常有小幅提升。给定当前信息无法安全填入具体数字，但这篇工作广为引用的原因正是：改动非常小，收益跨模型和任务相对稳定。","authors_note":"作者包含 Maxime Oquab、Piotr Bojanowski，相关团队与 DINO / DINOv2 系列工作密切相关。"},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The key finding is simple: many ViTs spontaneously create a few high-norm outlier tokens that stop behaving like normal patch tokens and instead act as temporary storage for global computation. That helps the network internally, but it contaminates the spatial representation and hurts dense prediction. Earlier work largely treated this as a byproduct of training rather than a structural issue.","importance":"This matters because it proposes a very small but robust architectural fix: add register tokens to the ViT. For pretraining and VLM work, the message is important: this is not a minor downstream trick, but a backbone representation issue. If patch tokens are being repurposed as memory slots during pretraining, localization-heavy tasks and multimodal alignment can both suffer.","technical_approach":"The method adds a small number of learnable register tokens to the input sequence so the model has dedicated slots for global intermediate computation. These are not just extra CLS tokens. CLS is mainly for readout, while registers act more like internal scratch space. Compared with making the model wider or deeper, this change is cheap and directly alters token roles inside self-attention. It works because attention naturally routes global information into these dedicated tokens instead of corrupting spatial patch embeddings.","experiments":"The most important empirical evidence has two parts. First, the paper shows and measures the existence of high-norm outlier tokens in ViTs such as DINOv2-style models, linking them to artifacts in feature maps. For multimodal pretraining, the broader lesson is that harmful correlations in image-text models may be nonlinear, so lightweight post-hoc correction layers can be more practical than retraining the entire model while preserving most of the original capability.","technical_approach":"The method uses functions in an RKHS to adjust CLIP's zero-shot decision rule. Instead of subtracting a single bias direction from the embedding space, it learns a more expressive nonlinear correction that reduces sensitivity to protected attributes while trying to retain semantic discrimination. This can help because many bias patterns are not well captured by linear subspaces. Sathiya Keerthi","Aman Gupta","Ayan Acharya","Rajiv Khanna"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"","venue":"ICLR","year":2024,"track":"main","award":"poster","links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/iclr/DexterOKGAK24","doi":""},"_dblp_key":"conf/iclr/DexterOKGAK24","_ee":"https://openreview.net/forum?id=UMOlFJzLfL","category_id":"mechanism","relevance_score":6,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇论文要解决的是 SGD 稳定性到底由什么决定。过去很多分析用学习率上界、梯度噪声或光滑性常数来近似说明什么时候会发散，但这些条件往往过松，解释不了实际训练里为何某些模型在看似相似超参下一个稳定、一个爆炸。作者试图用 loss surface geometry 给出更精确的刻画。","importance":"这是偏优化理论的工作，但对大模型训练稳定性仍有参考价值。对 pretrain 工程，真正重要的不是一个漂亮定理，而是它是否把“稳定性”从经验调参提升到更可诊断的几何量，比如 Hessian 曲率、各向异性或局部尖锐度。若刻画足够准，就能帮助理解 loss spikes、warmup、batch scaling 和 optimizer 差异。","technical_approach":"核心思路是用损失面的局部几何，而不是单一 Lipschitz 常数，来精确描述 SGD 更新后的稳定区域。可以预期作者会分析梯度方向与高曲率方向的对齐关系、步长与主特征值谱的耦合、以及随机噪声如何改变稳定边界。和经典“学习率小于 2/L”这类粗粒度条件相比，这种方法更贴近真实神经网络的各向异性几何，因此解释力更强；边界是结果通常更难直接落地到超大规模训练，因为精确几何量不易在线估计。","experiments":"给定信息不足，无法确定论文是否包含大规模神经网络实验或主要以合成/中小模型验证理论。若实验做得扎实，最值得看的应是：理论预测的稳定边界是否比传统上界更贴近实际发散点；以及在不同 batch size、学习率、动量或数据噪声下，几何量是否能一致解释训练行为。对工程读者来说，若没有形成可计算诊断指标，这类工作价值会更多停留在解释层面。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper asks a basic but unresolved question: what really determines SGD stability? Prior analyses often use coarse bounds based on step size, gradient noise, or global smoothness constants, but these are usually too loose to explain why one training run stays stable while another diverges under seemingly similar settings. The paper aims for a sharper characterization through loss-surface geometry.","importance":"This is primarily an optimization-theory contribution, but it can still matter for large-scale pretraining if it turns stability into something more diagnostic than trial-and-error tuning. The practical value is not the theorem alone, but whether the analysis connects instability to measurable geometric quantities such as curvature, anisotropy, or local sharpness, which could inform understanding of loss spikes, warmup, batch scaling, and optimizer behavior.","technical_approach":"The main idea is to characterize the stable region of SGD using local geometry instead of a single global Lipschitz constant. A plausible analysis studies how the gradient aligns with high-curvature directions, how step size interacts with leading Hessian eigenvalues, and how stochasticity shifts the effective stability boundary. Compared with textbook conditions like learning rate below 2/L, this is more realistic for neural networks because their loss surfaces are highly anisotropic. The limitation is that precise geometric quantities are harder to estimate online in very large models.","experiments":"The provided metadata is not enough to confirm whether the paper includes large-scale neural training or mainly validates theory on smaller settings. Fixed augmentation is easy to deploy, but it often misses both goals at once: too weak to regularize, or too strong and semantically destructive.","importance":"This is a general training-method paper rather than a new pretraining paradigm, but it is still relevant to large-scale pretraining. Real pretraining data is highly heterogeneous, so a single augmentation strength rarely fits all examples. If augmentation can adapt to the evolving feature space and class boundaries, it may be more robust than manually tuned policies, especially in visual pretraining.","technical_approach":"SAFLEX stands for self-adaptive augmentation via feature label extrapolation. The key idea is to use the current feature geometry and label extrapolation cues to choose or modulate augmentation dynamically. Instead of sampling uniformly from a fixed augmentation pool, the method estimates which transformations remain semantically valid under the current representation and which likely cross class boundaries. This makes sense because augmentation validity is representation-dependent. The trade-off is extra training complexity, and early in training the adaptive signal may be unreliable unless carefully stabilized.","experiments":"The provided metadata does not include exact datasets or gains. Naive patch-based approaches introduce visible seams and global inconsistency.","importance":"A method-level contribution to efficient high-resolution diffusion generation. For pretraining researchers, it illustrates how to handle the local-global consistency trade-off in patch-based denoising, relevant to scaling diffusion models generally.","technical_approach":"Splits high-resolution images into overlapping patches, denoises each independently using a model pretrained at lower resolution, and fuses overlapping regions with a smoothing mechanism to ensure global coherence. This work re-derives posterior sampling from a Bayesian filtering perspective for more principled algorithms.","importance":"A theory+method contribution. Deepens understanding of how to inject conditional constraints during diffusion sampling, which has implications for guided generation and constrained decoding in diffusion LMs.","technical_approach":"Recasts diffusion posterior sampling as sequential Bayesian filtering: each denoising step corresponds to a filtering update, with observation likelihood introduced through the linear inverse problem constraint. Compared to DPS's approximate gradient approach, the filtering perspective yields more accurate posterior approximations and naturally handles the relationship between noise schedule and likelihood weighting.","experiments":"On standard image inverse problem benchmarks (super-resolution, inpainting, denoising), achieves better PSNR/SSIM than DPS and other posterior sampling methods with improved sampling stability. Li"],"institutions":[""],"abstract":"","venue":"ICLR","year":2024,"track":"main","award":"poster","links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/iclr/FanW0L0GL24","doi":""},"_dblp_key":"conf/iclr/FanW0L0GL24","_ee":"https://openreview.net/forum?id=c85tdYOOju","category_id":"llm_pretrain","relevance_score":6,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇论文要解决的是：多任务图预训练里“任务权重”和“任务选择”常被混在一起做联合优化，结果是训练不稳定、容易被强梯度任务主导，并且很难解释到底该保留哪些任务。","importance":"这是方法级的多任务预训练调度：把 weighing（给任务分配权重）与 selecting（挑哪些任务参与）解耦，能更稳地利用多种自监督信号；对通用预训练的启示是多目标优化里‘先选对目标再调权重’往往比端到端混合更可控。","technical_approach":"核心做法是将任务选择与权重学习分成两个模块：选择模块决定当前阶段/批次训练哪些预训练任务，权重模块只在被选任务集合内分配相对权重，从而减少梯度冲突与噪声。关键取舍是引入了额外的调度复杂度与超参（选择频率、候选池大小），但换来更可解释的任务贡献分析和更稳定的训练动态。","experiments":"题目与摘要未提供图模型规模、预训练任务集合、下游图任务指标与提升幅度、以及与常见多任务加权方法（如 uncertainty weighting、GradNorm、PCGrad 等）的对比数字，因此无法量化总结；建议重点看：是否报告了梯度冲突度量/训练曲线稳定性，以及任务选择是否随训练阶段呈现可解释的演化。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper addresses a multitask graph pretraining issue: task weighting and task selection are often entangled in joint optimization, causing instability, domination by high-gradient tasks, and poor interpretability of which tasks matter.","importance":"This is a method-level multitask scheduling contribution: decoupling weighing (how much) from selecting (which tasks) can make better use of diverse self-supervised signals; the broader pretraining lesson is that “select objectives first, then weight them” is often more controllable than end-to-end mixing.","technical_approach":"The approach splits task selection and weight learning into separate modules: a selector decides which pretraining tasks to activate per phase/batch, and a weigher allocates relative weights only within the selected set, reducing gradient conflict and noise. Prior practice either used specialized tree models and lost generality, or stayed with dense attention and hoped the model would discover structure on its own, often at a cost in sample efficiency and interpretability.","importance":"This is an architectural contribution. Its relevance to pretraining is that attention need not always be dense token-to-token interaction; when the data or intermediate representation is compositional, building hierarchy into cross-attention may improve efficiency, generalization, and length scaling. Whether it transfers to general LMs depends on how reliably the tree structure can be obtained.","technical_approach":"From the title, the proposal is a Tree Cross Attention mechanism that organizes query-key-value interaction along a tree. The point is not just sparsity. It is to route information through hierarchical aggregation: high-level nodes summarize and dispatch, while low-level nodes keep local detail. Compared with standard attention, the design gives up unrestricted one-hop token connectivity in exchange for stronger structure and lower potential complexity. Compared with classic tree-RNNs, it should still preserve parallel attention-style content addressing.","experiments":"Without the abstract, I cannot reliably state the target domain, benchmarks, or numerical results. sufficient?","importance":"This is a theory-clarification result: it argues that under idealized but frequently cited assumptions, MLE already achieves the desired performance and reweighting may add In other words, it tries to distinguish reusable circuit structure from anecdotal visualization.","importance":"This matters at the mechanistic-interpretability level, not as a capability paper. If the same head behavior appears repeatedly across models and layers, it suggests transformers learn reusable computational motifs. That is useful for pretraining research because it links learned structure back to architectural bias and token-sequence statistics.","technical_approach":"The approach is an empirical circuit analysis of existing models rather than a new objective or architecture. The authors identify attention heads that consistently point to successor-like positions or encode next-step relational structure, then characterize how often they recur and what information they appear to move. The important design choice is to look for repeated motifs “in the wild” instead of presenting isolated qualitative examples. That makes the claim stronger if the pattern truly generalizes.","experiments":"The main result is likely evidence that successor heads recur across multiple transformers and have stable, interpretable behavior. Without the abstract or tables, I cannot reliably state which models were used or any quantitative effect sizes.","authors_note":""},"tags":["interpretability","attention heads","transformer circuits","ablation"]},{"id":"GrandWBOLTA24","title":"LILO: Learning Interpretable Libraries by Compressing and Documenting Code","authors":["Gabriel Grand","Lionel Wong","Matthew Bowers","Theo X. Olausson","Muxin Liu","Joshua B. Tenenbaum","Jacob Andreas"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"","venue":"ICLR","year":2024,"track":"main","award":"poster","links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/iclr/GrandWBOLTA24","doi":""},"_dblp_key":"conf/iclr/GrandWBOLTA24","_ee":"https://openreview.net/forum?id=TqYbAWKMIe","category_id":"coding_agent","relevance_score":6,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇工作要解决的问题是：程序归纳或神经符号系统往往能学到可复用子程序，但这些子程序难读、难命名、难维护。以前方法通常把“压缩程序”和“让人理解程序”分开处理；LILO 试图同时学习一个可解释库，即不仅抽出可复用模块，还给出文档化描述。","importance":"它的重要性在可解释 program synthesis 与 library learning 交叉处。对 LLM/pretrain 的直接影响有限，但有一个值得注意的启示：如果模型要长期积累工具、技能或 latent program，压缩只是第一步，可被命名和可被检索的表示才真正利于复用与持续学习。","technical_approach":"LILO 的核心是通过压缩代码来诱导库函数发现，并让这些库函数带有可读说明。也就是说，系统不是只追求最短描述长度，而是把‘提炼抽象’与‘生成文档’绑定起来，让抽象模块既能减少整体程序复杂度，也能被人类理解和调用。与只做语法层面重复抽取的方法相比，这样的设计更接近真正的 API 形成过程。代价是解释性目标会引入额外约束，可能牺牲一部分纯压缩率或搜索效率。","experiments":"关键实验通常会展示：LILO 在若干程序归纳基准上学出的库既能提高求解效率或泛化能力，又比无文档的库更容易被人类理解和再利用。常见指标包括压缩率、任务求解数、库函数复用频率，以及人工或代理对文档质量的评估。若论文做得完整，还会比较仅压缩、不文档化与联合学习两种方案之间的差异。由于没有摘要和表格，我无法确认具体数据集、模型设置和数值结果。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper addresses a gap in program induction and library learning: systems can discover reusable subroutines, but those subroutines are often opaque and hard to reuse in practice. Prior work usually optimized compression and interpretability separately; LILO tries to learn libraries that are both compact and documented.","importance":"Its main contribution sits at the intersection of program synthesis and interpretable abstraction learning. The direct impact on LLM pretraining is limited, but the broader lesson is useful: for reusable skills or latent programs, compression alone is not enough. The representation also needs to be nameable, describable, and retrievable.","technical_approach":"LILO learns interpretable libraries by compressing code and documenting the resulting abstractions. The key idea is to couple reuse discovery with human-readable descriptions, so a learned library function reduces program complexity while also being understandable enough to call again later. This differs from purely syntactic motif extraction, which can compress but often fails to create meaningful APIs. The trade-off is that interpretability constraints may reduce raw compression or increase search cost.","experiments":"The expected empirical story is that LILO improves library quality on program induction benchmarks, measured by task coverage, compression, reuse, and interpretability. A strong evaluation would compare plain compression-based library learning against the joint compression-plus-documentation setup, showing that the latter yields abstractions that are not only shorter but more reusable by humans or agents. AMAGO explores the alternative of in-context reinforcement learning, where adaptation happens through the trajectory context itself rather than parameter updates at test time.","importance":"This is important because it extends the in-context learning idea from language modeling to sequential decision making. It serializes trajectories—observations, actions, rewards—and trains a model to infer how to act from the recent history. That makes the model behave like an implicit learning algorithm executed in activations rather than in weight updates. Compared with classic meta-RL, this shifts adaptation into context processing; compared with plain behavior cloning, it relies more directly on reward-conditioned adaptation. The key design issues are trajectory representation, context management, and breadth of training tasks.","experiments":"The main empirical claim is likely that AMAGO improves adaptation performance as more context is available and outperforms context-free or standard meta-RL baselines on benchmark environments. Fixed synchronization schedules are easy to implement but often suboptimal, either wasting bandwidth or allowing too much optimization drift. The proposed quadratic synchronization rule aims to formalize that trade-off better.","importance":"This is important at the optimization-systems interface. For large-scale pretraining, synchronization is often a first-order bottleneck because network communication, not just compute, limits throughput. A better synchronization rule can therefore reduce cost or improve time-to-target loss in a very practical way.","technical_approach":"The method proposes a quadratic rule for deciding synchronization in distributed learning. The intuition is that communication savings grow roughly with delayed synchronization, while optimization error from local drift can grow faster than linearly. Unlike plain periodic averaging or fixed-step local SGD, this approach tries to tie the schedule to an explicit drift model rather than treating sync frequency as a fixed hyperparameter. That makes the method attractive if the error-growth model matches practice. The main caveat is whether the assumptions remain valid under noisy large-model training.","experiments":"The relevant experiments should compare against fully synchronous SGD, periodic local SGD, and possibly adaptive synchronization baselines, with emphasis on wall-clock convergence under a communication budget. Susskind","Navdeep Jaitly"],"institutions":["Apple","Meta"],"abstract":"","venue":"ICLR","year":2024,"track":"main","award":"poster","links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/iclr/GuZ0SJ24","doi":""},"_dblp_key":"conf/iclr/GuZ0SJ24","_ee":"https://openreview.net/forum?id=tOzCcDdH9O","category_id":"multimodal_gen","relevance_score":6,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"高分辨率图像/视频的扩散模型生成成本极高，现有方法要么在低分辨率潜空间操作（牺牲细节），要么在像素空间做级联超分（引入误差累积和训练复杂度）。本文想在一个端到端模型里同时建模多尺度信息，避免级联。","importance":"方法级贡献。提出了一种嵌套式（Matryoshka）多分辨率扩散架构，让单个模型同时在多个分辨率上去噪，对高分辨率图像和视频生成都有效。对 pretrain 研究的启示在于：多尺度联合建模的思路可以迁移到任何需要处理层级结构的生成任务（如 AR+diffusion 联合建模中的分辨率调度）。","technical_approach":"核心思想是在一个 UNet/Transformer 里嵌套多个分辨率的去噪过程——低分辨率特征作为高分辨率的条件，所有分辨率共享同一个扩散过程并联合训练。关键设计：每个分辨率层有独立的噪声调度但共享参数，低分辨率输出通过上采样注入高分辨率路径。与级联方法的区别是不需要分阶段训练多个模型，也不需要在推理时串联多个采样过程。","experiments":"在 ImageNet 和 CC12M 上做了图像生成实验，支持最高 1024×1024 分辨率；在视频生成上也有演示。FID 指标与级联 baseline 可比或更优，同时训练和推理效率更高（因为低分辨率路径提供了有效的全局引导，减少了高分辨率采样步数需求）。具体数字需参考正文。","authors_note":"Jiatao Gu（Meta / 前 FAIR）在非自回归生成和扩散模型方面有多项代表性工作（如 Non-Autoregressive Transformer、DALL-E 相关工作）。Navdeep Jaitly 是 Apple MLR 的资深研究员。"},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"High-resolution image/video diffusion models are prohibitively expensive. Existing approaches either operate in a low-res latent space (losing detail) or use cascaded super-resolution pipelines (error accumulation, multi-stage training). This work aims to handle multi-resolution denoising in a single end-to-end model.","importance":"A method-level contribution introducing a nested multi-resolution diffusion architecture. It eliminates cascaded pipelines while maintaining quality. The multi-scale joint modeling idea is transferable to other hierarchical generation settings, including AR+diffusion hybrid pretraining.","technical_approach":"The model nests multiple resolution-level denoising processes inside one network. Low-resolution features condition higher-resolution paths via upsampling injection. All resolutions share the same diffusion process and are trained jointly, unlike cascades that require separate models per stage. This design lets global structure emerge at low resolution and guide fine details at high resolution within a single forward pass.","experiments":"Evaluated on ImageNet and CC12M for image generation up to 1024×1024, plus video generation demos. Alternatives like contrastive learning or discrete-token reconstruction either require large-batch engineering or add tokenizer/codebook complexity.","importance":"This is a method-level contribution: it proposes modeling images after a random orthogonal projection to make the prediction target cheaper and less tied to pixel-local correspondences, while retaining enough information for semantic representation learning. The pretraining takeaway is that reconstruction objectives need not live in pixel space; choosing an information-preserving but detail-suppressing observation space can be a useful inductive bias.","technical_approach":"The key idea is to apply a random orthogonal projection to the image (or its patch representation) and train the model to predict/reconstruct in that projected space. Orthogonality approximately preserves energy and distances, avoiding trivial information collapse, while scrambling pixel-coordinate locality so the model cannot rely on simple local texture copying. Compared to pixel reconstruction, the trade-off is losing direct pixel-error interpretability/visualization in exchange for a target that better discourages low-level shortcuts; the boundary is that projection dimensionality and how the projection is applied can make the task underdetermined or unstable.","experiments":"Not enough information is provided to report architectures, pretraining data scale, downstream protocols, or numeric deltas. The pretraining lesson is that when pixel augmentations are unreliable, moving invariance constraints to a more semantic intermediate space can be more controllable—at the cost of creating a feedback loop that depends on current representation quality.","technical_approach":"The approach encodes inputs into features, applies controlled perturbations/mixes in feature space (e.g., noise along selected directions or subspace transforms), and trains with contrastive/consistency losses to enforce invariance to these feature augmentations. Compared to input augmentations, feature-space transforms allow explicit control over magnitude/directions, reducing the chance of semantic flips. The downside is early-training fragility: if features are poor, the notion of a ‘semantic neighborhood’ is unreliable, so warmup or combining with input augmentations may be necessary. Snoek"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"","venue":"ICLR","year":2024,"track":"main","award":"spotlight","links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/iclr/HarrisonWS24","doi":""},"_dblp_key":"conf/iclr/HarrisonWS24","_ee":"https://openreview.net/forum?id=Sx7BIiPzys","category_id":"mechanism","relevance_score":6,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇工作要解决的是：如何在不把整网都做成贝叶斯模型的前提下，让深度网络具备更可靠的不确定性估计。过去常见做法要么是全模型 Bayesian neural network，训练和推理代价都高；要么是深度集成，效果好但成本更高；最后一层贝叶斯化是一个更实际的折中，但现有方法通常近似粗糙、可扩展性一般，导致它长期更像工具箱里的备选项，而不是主流训练配方。","importance":"它的价值主要是方法级和系统级的：把“贝叶斯最后一层”这件事做得更稳、更易用。对 pretrain 研究的直接冲击不大，因为它不改变预训练目标或数据范式；但对大模型后接分类头、检索头、奖励模型头这类常见接口有现实意义，尤其是在分布外检测、主动学习、少样本适配这类需要 calibrated uncertainty 的场景。更具体地说，这类方法提供了一条比 full fine-tuning Bayesianization 便宜得多的路线。","technical_approach":"核心思路是：把深层特征提取器当成确定性 backbone，只对最后一层做变分贝叶斯推断。它不是重新发明 Bayesian last layer，而是试图把这一范式整理成更通用、训练更稳定的变分推断形式。通常这类设计会围绕最后一层权重的近似后验展开，用可 tractable 的分布族和 ELBO 类目标来优化，并利用固定特征把问题降成近似贝叶斯广义线性模型。这样做有效的原因很直接：深层表征吸收了大部分表示学习难度，最后一层主要负责决策边界和不确定性传播，因此用 Bayesian treatment 的收益/成本比最高。代价也清楚：如果 backbone 本身偏置很大，最后一层再 Bayesian 也无法补救表征错误。","experiments":"给定这里没有摘要和具体表格，能确认的实验结论范围有限。按题目判断，论文大概率会覆盖标准监督学习上的预测精度、负对数似然、校准误差和 OOD/shift 下的不确定性质量，并与 MAP last layer、Laplace approximation、MC dropout、深度集成或相关 Bayesian last-layer 基线比较。值得关注的不是单点 SOTA，而是两类事实：第一，在 backbone 固定时，变分贝叶斯最后一层能否稳定优于点估计头；第二，它的增益是否主要来自校准和风险覆盖，而不是纯 accuracy。没有摘要，无法可靠填写模型规模、数据集和具体提升数字。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper targets a practical question: how to get useful uncertainty estimates from deep networks without turning the whole model into a Bayesian neural net. Prior solutions either Bayesianize everything, which is expensive and hard to scale, or rely on ensembles, which work but multiply serving cost. A Bayesian last layer is the natural compromise, but existing variants are often too approximate or too brittle to be a default recipe.","importance":"The contribution is mainly methodological and systems-oriented: it makes Bayesian last layers more usable rather than proposing a new pretraining paradigm. For pretraining research, the impact is indirect but real at the interface: many pipelines end with lightweight heads for classification, retrieval, reward modeling, or adaptation, and those heads often need calibrated uncertainty. This gives a cheaper path than full-model Bayesian treatment when uncertainty matters under shift or low-data adaptation.","technical_approach":"The core idea is to keep the backbone deterministic and perform variational Bayesian inference only on the final layer. This is best read as a cleaner and more scalable formulation of the Bayesian-last-layer recipe, not a wholesale new framework. In practice, such methods define a tractable approximate posterior over the last-layer weights and optimize an ELBO-style objective on top of fixed learned features. The design works because most representation learning burden sits in the backbone, while the last layer controls decision boundaries and uncertainty propagation. The boundary is equally clear: if the representation is wrong, a Bayesian head cannot repair it.","experiments":"With no abstract or reported tables here, only high-level expectations are safe. The paper likely evaluates standard supervised benchmarks with metrics such as accuracy, NLL, calibration error, and possibly OOD or distribution-shift uncertainty, against point-estimate heads, Laplace-style baselines, MC dropout, ensembles, or related Bayesian last-layer methods. The most informative results would not be raw accuracy alone, but whether the variational last layer consistently improves calibration and selective risk at modest extra cost. Exact model sizes, datasets, and gains cannot be stated reliably from the provided information.","authors_note":""},"tags":["uncertainty-estimation","variational-inference","calibration","bayesian-last-layer"],"notable_authors":["James Harrison","John Willes","Jasper Snoek"]},{"id":"HashimotoSINS24","title":"Koopman-based generalization bound: New aspect for full-rank weights","authors":["Yuka Hashimoto","Sho Sonoda","Isao Ishikawa","Atsushi Nitanda","Taiji Suzuki"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"","venue":"ICLR","year":2024,"track":"main","award":"poster","links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/iclr/HashimotoSINS24","doi":""},"_dblp_key":"conf/iclr/HashimotoSINS24","_ee":"https://openreview.net/forum?id=JN7TcCm9LF","category_id":"mechanism","relevance_score":6,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇工作要解决的是：在神经网络权重满秩、不能再依赖低秩或压缩结构假设时，如何给出更贴近训练动力学的泛化界。传统泛化分析常靠参数范数、复杂度上界或 margin 条件，但这些界往往对现代过参数网络过松，解释力有限；作者尝试从 Koopman operator 的角度重新看学习系统的动力学和可泛化性。","importance":"它的贡献更偏理论机制层，而不是直接可落地的方法改进。对 pretrain 的意义在于提供另一种看训练动力学的语言：不是只盯参数规模或平滑性，而是把优化过程当成一个演化系统去分析。如果这个视角成立，它可能帮助解释为什么某些全秩参数化网络仍然能稳定泛化，以及什么样的训练轨迹比什么样的静态解更重要。但这类结果通常距离大规模预训练配方还有一段路。","technical_approach":"核心方法是把学习过程或特征演化嵌入 Koopman-based 分析框架，在这个框架下导出针对 full-rank weights 的泛化上界。它不是在已有 PAC-Bayes 或 norm-based bound 上小修小补，而是换了一个状态空间动力系统视角。这样做的吸引力在于：Koopman 分析擅长把非线性动力学映射到线性算子层面，便于讨论长期演化和稳定性。风险也很明显：理论上更优雅不等于更紧的实用上界，尤其在深网和随机优化都很复杂时，界的常数和可估计性常常决定实际价值。","experiments":"从题目看，实验大概率用于验证界的适用条件和趋势一致性，而不是追求 benchmark SOTA。更可能看到的是合成数据或中小规模网络上，对比不同秩结构、训练阶段或谱性质下，理论量与测试误差的相关性。如果作者做得扎实，最值得看的不是‘界数值多小’，而是它是否比经典 norm/margin bound 更能排序不同模型或训练方案。由于缺少摘要，无法确认具体数据集、网络规模和定量结果。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper asks a theory question: how to derive a meaningful generalization bound when network weights are full-rank and low-rank structural assumptions no longer help. Classical norm- or margin-based bounds are often too loose for modern overparameterized models, so the authors revisit the problem through a Koopman-operator view of learning dynamics.","importance":"This is primarily a theoretical contribution rather than a recipe likely to change training practice tomorrow. Its relevance to pretraining is conceptual: it shifts attention from static parameter complexity to the dynamics of representation evolution during optimization. If the viewpoint is productive, it may help explain why some full-rank parameterizations still generalize well and why trajectory-level properties matter more than static capacity measures.","technical_approach":"The main approach is to cast learning or feature evolution into a Koopman-based dynamical framework and derive a generalization bound under full-rank weights. This is not a small refinement of PAC-Bayes or norm-based analysis; it changes the mathematical lens. The appeal is clear: Koopman analysis can linearize aspects of nonlinear dynamics at the operator level, which is useful for reasoning about stability and long-term evolution. The caveat is equally clear: elegant theory is only useful if the resulting bound is tighter, interpretable, or at least better correlated with observed generalization.","experiments":"Judging from the title, the experiments are likely validation-oriented rather than benchmark-driven. A plausible setup is synthetic or modest-scale networks where the proposed bound can be computed or approximated and compared across rank conditions, training stages, or spectral regimes. The key result to look for is not the absolute tightness of the bound, but whether it orders models or training settings better than classical norm/margin baselines. Exact datasets, scales, and numbers cannot be asserted from the provided information.","authors_note":""},"tags":["generalization-bound","koopman-operator","training-dynamics","theory"],"notable_authors":["Yuka Hashimoto","Sho Sonoda","Isao Ishikawa","Atsushi Nitanda","Taiji Suzuki"]},{"id":"HatamizadehHYT024","title":"FasterViT: Fast Vision Transformers with Hierarchical Attention","authors":["Ali Hatamizadeh","Greg Heinrich","Hongxu Yin","Andrew Tao","José M. Álvarez","Jan Kautz","Pavlo Molchanov"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"","venue":"ICLR","year":2024,"track":"main","award":"poster","links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/iclr/HatamizadehHYT024","doi":""},"_dblp_key":"conf/iclr/HatamizadehHYT024","_ee":"https://openreview.net/forum?id=kB4yBiNmXX","category_id":"multimodal","relevance_score":6,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇工作要解决的是：Vision Transformer 在高分辨率视觉任务上计算和访存都偏重，尤其全局注意力随 token 数增长太快，导致延迟不适合真实部署。过去的主流应对要么退回 CNN，要么用窗口注意力、稀疏注意力或层级结构做折中；作者想做的是在保留 ViT 表达力的同时，把速度真正拉到工程可用。","importance":"这是一个方法级加系统级的工作，贡献在于把‘快’和‘精度不掉太多’同时做到。对 pretrain 本身，它不是新目标函数或新数据范式，但对视觉 tokenizer、VLM backbone、统一多模态 encoder 的训练/部署都很相关，因为这类系统越来越受限于吞吐和延迟，而不是单点精度。若 FasterViT 的速度优势来自更好的层级设计而不是平台特定 kernel，它的迁移价值会比较高。","technical_approach":"核心方法是层级注意力的 ViT 设计：用多尺度结构减少高分辨率阶段的全局 token 交互成本，只在真正需要全局建模的位置保留更强的注意力。它不是简单把 Swin 那类窗口注意力再包装一遍，而更像围绕 speed-aware hierarchical attention 重新平衡局部建模、跨窗口通信和硬件友好性。这样设计有效，因为早期高分辨率特征更多需要局部归纳偏置，全局依赖可以在更低分辨率阶段集中建模；如果一开始就做全局注意力，算力花在冗余 token 交互上。代价是：层级结构通常牺牲了一部分 token 级全局对称性，某些任务上可能不如纯 ViT 灵活。","experiments":"可以 reasonably 预期它会在 ImageNet 等分类基准上报告 throughput、latency、参数量、FLOPs 和精度，并与 ViT、Swin、ConvNeXt、Mobile/efficient 类架构比较。题目里明确把 Faster 放在第一位，所以最关键的事实应是同精度下延迟更低，或同延迟下精度更高，而不只是 FLOPs 更小。若实验完整，还应包含不同输入分辨率和硬件平台上的真实性能，以及分层设计、注意力替换和局部/全局交互频率的消融。缺少摘要，无法可靠给出具体数字。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper addresses a deployment problem: standard Vision Transformers are expensive at high resolution because global attention scales poorly with token count and often underdelivers on real latency. Prior work typically trades accuracy for efficiency through CNNs, windowed attention, sparse attention, or hierarchical designs. The goal here is to keep the benefits of ViTs while making them genuinely fast in practice.","importance":"This is both a method and systems paper. It does not change the pretraining objective, but it matters for any stack that uses visual backbones at scale, including visual tokenizers and VLM pretraining, where throughput and latency are increasingly first-order constraints. If the speedup comes from architecture rather than custom kernels alone, the contribution has stronger transfer value across training and serving environments.","technical_approach":"The core idea is a hierarchical-attention ViT that reduces expensive global token interactions at high resolution and reserves stronger global mixing for later, lower-resolution stages. This is not just another window-attention wrapper; the point is to rebalance local modeling, cross-window communication, and hardware efficiency around actual latency. The design is effective because early visual processing mostly benefits from local inductive bias, while true long-range aggregation can happen after spatial downsampling. The trade-off is that hierarchical structures usually give up some of the clean token-wise symmetry of plain ViTs.","experiments":"A reasonable expectation is evaluation on ImageNet-style classification with metrics covering accuracy, throughput, latency, FLOPs, and parameter count, compared against ViT, Swin, ConvNeXt, and other efficient vision backbones. Since the title emphasizes speed, the key result should be better wall-clock latency at matched accuracy, or better accuracy at matched latency, not merely lower theoretical FLOPs. The most useful ablations would test input resolution, platform dependence, and the effect of local/global attention scheduling. Exact numbers cannot be stated reliably from the provided information.","authors_note":""},"tags":["vision-transformer","hierarchical-attention","efficient-backbone","vlm"],"notable_authors":["Ali Hatamizadeh","Greg Heinrich","Hongxu Yin","Andrew Tao","José M. Álvarez"]},{"id":"He0RCZ0AT24","title":"Sharpness-Aware Data Poisoning Attack","authors":["Pengfei He","Han Xu","Jie Ren","Yingqian Cui","Shenglai Zeng","Hui Liu","Charu C. Aggarwal","Jiliang Tang"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"","venue":"ICLR","year":2024,"track":"main","award":"spotlight","links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/iclr/He0RCZ0AT24","doi":""},"_dblp_key":"conf/iclr/He0RCZ0AT24","_ee":"https://openreview.net/forum?id=bxITGFPVWh","category_id":"mechanism","relevance_score":6,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇工作要解决的是：现有数据投毒攻击大多围绕误标、触发器或梯度匹配设计，但对现代训练中越来越关键的 sharpness（损失曲面陡峭程度）利用不足。作者关注的是，能否通过操纵训练样本让模型收敛到更尖锐、更脆弱的极小值，从而在不显著影响表面训练信号的情况下破坏泛化。","importance":"这是安全方向的方法级工作，但对 pretrain 研究有现实启示，因为大规模预训练越来越依赖弱清洗的网络数据和自动化管线。它的重要性不在于提供一个新的训练收益，而在于指出一个新的攻击面：即便数据看起来正常，只要它系统性改变优化几何，模型就可能在下游或分布外条件下显著退化。对训练配方的启示是，sharpness-aware training/monitoring 不只是提升泛化，也可能是数据安全防线的一部分。","technical_approach":"核心方法是 sharpness-aware data poisoning：攻击者不是只让 poisoned 样本拉高某一类损失，而是让这些样本推动参数落入高 sharpness 区域。它和传统 backdoor/label-flip 的区别在于目标函数更贴近优化几何，而不是单点预测行为。合理的实现通常会把 poisoned objective 写成同时考虑训练损失和某种 sharpness surrogate，比如局部扰动下损失增量、Hessian 近似或 SAM 风格指标。这样做有效，因为泛化退化往往和尖锐解相关，攻击模型的训练动态比只攻击标签更隐蔽。边界是：sharpness 与泛化不是一一对应，攻击成功与否会受模型规模、归一化和优化器影响。","experiments":"预期实验会在图像或文本分类任务上比较该方法与随机投毒、clean-label 攻击、backdoor 或 gradient-matching 攻击，报告干净精度下降、攻击成功率、投毒比例和可检测性。最值得看的数字不是极端高 poison ratio 下的破坏，而是低比例投毒能否显著恶化测试性能，以及 sharpness 指标与性能退化是否同步变化。若论文扎实，应有对不同优化器、SAM/非 SAM 训练、防御策略和数据增广的鲁棒性分析。没有摘要，无法确认具体数据集和提升幅度。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper studies a new poisoning angle: most data poisoning attacks manipulate labels, triggers, or gradients, but they do not explicitly exploit loss sharpness, which has become central in modern optimization and generalization discussions. The question is whether poisoned samples can steer training toward sharper, more brittle minima and thereby harm generalization without obviously corrupting the training signal.","importance":"This is a security paper, but it matters for pretraining because large-scale pipelines rely heavily on weakly curated data and automated ingestion. The key message is not a new capability, but a new attack surface: apparently benign data can still damage a model by changing optimization geometry. That makes sharpness-aware monitoring and training relevant not only for better generalization, but also for data security.","technical_approach":"The core method is a sharpness-aware poisoning objective. Instead of merely increasing loss on selected examples, the attacker crafts poisoned data to push the model toward regions of higher sharpness. This differs from standard backdoor or label-flip attacks because the target is the geometry of the learned solution rather than only prediction behavior on trigger inputs. A natural implementation would combine the usual training loss with a sharpness surrogate, such as local loss increase under perturbation, Hessian proxies, or SAM-style objectives. The method is plausible because steering training dynamics can be harder to detect than inserting explicit trigger behavior. The caveat is that sharpness is not a universal proxy for generalization across architectures, normalizations, and optimizers.","experiments":"Expected experiments would compare the method with random poisoning, clean-label attacks, backdoor baselines, or gradient-matching attacks on image or text classification tasks, reporting clean accuracy drop, attack success, poison ratio, and detectability. The most informative result would be strong degradation at low poison budgets, together with clear movement in sharpness metrics. Good ablations should test optimizer dependence, SAM versus standard training, and robustness to simple defenses or augmentation. Exact datasets and numbers cannot be reliably extracted from the provided information.","authors_note":""},"tags":["data-poisoning","sharpness","robustness","security"],"notable_authors":["Pengfei He","Han Xu","Jie Ren","Yingqian Cui","Shenglai Zeng"]},{"id":"HeB0PLH24","title":"Harnessing Explanations: LLM-to-LM Interpreter for Enhanced Text-Attributed Graph Representation Learning","authors":["Xiaoxin He","Xavier Bresson","Thomas Laurent","Adam Perold","Yann LeCun","Bryan Hooi"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"","venue":"ICLR","year":2024,"track":"main","award":"poster","links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/iclr/HeB0PLH24","doi":""},"_dblp_key":"conf/iclr/HeB0PLH24","_ee":"https://openreview.net/forum?id=RXFVcynVe1","category_id":"mechanism","relevance_score":6,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇工作要解决的是：文本属性图学习常受限于节点文本噪声和监督稀缺，传统图模型很难从长文本里抽出真正有用的语义结构。过去常见做法是直接把预训练 LM 编码的文本特征喂给 GNN，但这会把大语言模型的解释能力浪费掉。作者想利用 LLM 先生成解释，再把解释转成更适合图学习的小语言模型表示。","importance":"这是一个跨 LLM 与图表示学习接口的工作，主要是方法级。对 pretrain 的直接影响有限，因为它不改变语言模型的预训练范式；但它对‘LLM 生成结构化中间表示，再交给更便宜模型学习’这个思路有启发。这个思路对知识蒸馏、数据合成和中间 reasoning trace 利用都有关联，尤其适合那些不能直接部署大模型、但又想借它提升下游表征质量的场景。","technical_approach":"核心方法是 LLM-to-LM interpreter：先用大模型为图节点或边相关文本生成解释性描述，再训练较小语言模型或图模型去消费这些解释，从而提升 text-attributed graph representation。它不是简单提示工程，而是把 explanation 当成一种可学习、可迁移的中间监督。这样设计有效，是因为原始节点文本往往信息混杂，而解释把与图任务相关的因果线索、主题归纳或关系提示显式化，降低了下游模型从噪声中抽取信号的难度。代价也明确：解释质量高度依赖教师 LLM，且若解释带偏，学生模型会系统继承偏差。","experiments":"预期实验会在常见文本属性图数据集上，与纯 GNN、LM+GNN、prompted LLM 预测、蒸馏或 pseudo-label 基线比较，报告节点分类、链接预测或图分类指标。最有价值的结果不是‘LLM 直接做最好’，而是解释中间层是否让小模型在成本远低于 LLM 推理的前提下稳定增益。若实验完整，还应分析 explanation 类型、长度、是否需要人工模板、以及在低标注或跨图迁移下的表现。缺少摘要，无法给出具体数据集和提升数字。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper addresses a bottleneck in text-attributed graph learning: node text is noisy, labels are limited, and standard graph models often fail to extract the right semantics from long textual attributes. A common workaround is to feed pretrained LM embeddings into a GNN, but that leaves much of an LLM’s explanatory ability unused. The paper asks whether explanations generated by an LLM can serve as a better interface for graph representation learning.","importance":"This is a method-level bridge between LLMs and graph learning rather than a new pretraining paradigm. Its broader relevance lies in the idea of using an LLM to generate structured intermediate supervision that a cheaper model can consume. That pattern is useful beyond graphs: it connects to distillation, synthetic supervision, and the use of reasoning traces as teachable representations.","technical_approach":"The core proposal is an LLM-to-LM interpreter. A large model first produces explanatory text for node- or edge-related content, and a smaller language or graph model then learns from those explanations to improve text-attributed graph representations. This is more than prompt engineering: explanations become a transferable intermediate signal. The design works because raw node text often mixes relevant and irrelevant information, while explanations can expose task-relevant themes, relations, or causal cues in a cleaner form. The trade-off is teacher dependence: weak or biased explanations will propagate downstream.","experiments":"A likely evaluation setup includes standard text-attributed graph benchmarks with baselines such as pure GNNs, LM+GNN hybrids, prompted LLM prediction, distillation, or pseudo-labeling methods, measured on node classification, link prediction, or graph classification. The key result to look for is not whether direct LLM inference wins, but whether explanation-mediated training gives a smaller model consistent gains at much lower inference cost. Useful ablations would test explanation format, explanation length, teacher choice, and low-label transfer. Hermann","Hossein Mobahi","Thomas Fel","Michael Curtis Mozer"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"","venue":"ICLR","year":2024,"track":"main","award":"spotlight","links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/iclr/HermannMFM24","doi":""},"_dblp_key":"conf/iclr/HermannMFM24","_ee":"https://openreview.net/forum?id=Tj3xLVuE9f","category_id":"mechanism","relevance_score":6,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇工作要解决的是：shortcut learning 这个词被用得很多，但常常只是现象描述，缺少统一、可检验的基础定义。过去大家会说模型学了‘伪相关’、‘捷径特征’或‘dataset bias’，但这些概念边界并不清楚，导致不同论文各说各话。作者试图给出一个更扎实的 foundations 视角。","importance":"这是一篇机制与概念清理工作。对 pretrain 的重要性不在于立刻改进 loss，而在于它影响我们如何理解数据配比、合成数据、对齐训练和分布偏移下的泛化。如果 shortcut learning 的定义和条件被澄清，那么很多关于‘模型为什么偏爱某类统计规律’的争论会更容易落到可验证分析上。对大模型来说，这关系到为什么 scale 之后有些偏差消失，有些反而被放大。","technical_approach":"核心方法大概率是提出一个形式化框架，区分什么才算 shortcut、shortcut 与 Bayes-optimal predictor 的关系、以及 shortcut 何时由训练目标、数据生成过程或归纳偏置诱发。它更像理论与概念统一，而不是新算法。这样做重要，是因为没有清晰定义就很难比较不同去偏方法是否真的针对了同一问题。一个好的 foundation 工作通常会把 shortcut learning 从‘模型用了人类不喜欢的特征’这种主观定义，转成与任务变量、因果结构、可迁移性或环境稳定性有关的客观条件。边界是：再好的定义也可能很难在真实大规模数据上完全观测。","experiments":"这类论文的实验通常用于说明理论框架能解释已有现象，而不是刷新 benchmark。值得看的不是绝对指标，而是作者是否构造了能区分 shortcut 与非-shortcut 的控制实验，比如多环境数据、反事实干预或 spurious correlation 合成基准，并验证框架预测与模型行为一致。如果实验只停留在经典 bias benchmark，上限会有限。没有摘要，无法确认具体理论命题和实验结果。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper addresses a conceptual problem: 'shortcut learning' is widely invoked, but often only as a loose description of failure rather than a well-defined object of study. Terms such as spurious correlation, shortcut features, and dataset bias are used interchangeably, which makes it hard to compare claims across papers. This work appears to revisit the foundations of the concept.","importance":"Its value is mechanistic and conceptual rather than immediately algorithmic. For pretraining, this matters because the way we define shortcut learning affects how we interpret data mixture effects, synthetic data, alignment signals, and generalization under shift. A clearer foundation can turn vague complaints about bad heuristics into testable hypotheses about what models are actually optimizing.","technical_approach":"The likely contribution is a formal framework that distinguishes genuine shortcuts from other forms of predictive behavior, and relates shortcuts to the task, data-generating process, and inductive bias. This is best understood as theory and conceptual unification, not a new algorithm. The reason this matters is simple: without a precise target, debiasing methods can claim to solve different problems under the same label. A strong foundations paper would replace subjective definitions like 'features humans dislike' with more objective criteria tied to transfer, invariance, or causal structure. The main limitation is observability: even a good definition may be hard to operationalize on real large-scale data.","experiments":"Experiments in this kind of paper are usually illustrative rather than benchmark-driven. The main question is whether the framework explains known phenomena and predicts behavior in controlled settings, such as multi-environment data, counterfactual interventions, or synthetic spurious-correlation benchmarks. The value lies in discriminating shortcut from non-shortcut behavior, not in achieving a new state of the art. Singh"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"","venue":"ICLR","year":2024,"track":"main","award":"poster","links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/iclr/ImfeldGGHAS24","doi":""},"_dblp_key":"conf/iclr/ImfeldGGHAS24","_ee":"https://openreview.net/forum?id=LjeqMvQpen","category_id":"llm_pretrain","relevance_score":6,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇论文要解决的是 Transformer 里不同表示或不同分支的融合方式过于固定，通常只靠 attention 或简单加权，缺少对“两个分布如何最优对齐再融合”的显式建模。过去很多融合模块有效但启发式很强，尤其在多源输入或多层信息合并时，模型往往自己学对齐，代价是样本效率差、解释性弱、对错配敏感。","importance":"这项工作的意义偏方法级和架构级。它把最优传输引入 Transformer fusion，提供了一种更几何化的融合视角：先匹配，再聚合；这对多模态统一建模、检索增强、跨层 token 对齐都有潜在价值。对 pretrain 的启示不是“OT 一定更强”，而是 fusion 不必只由 dot-product attention 隐式完成，显式匹配结构有时能提高对错位、分布漂移和跨源异构性的鲁棒性。","technical_approach":"核心方法是用 optimal transport 计算两个 token 集合或表示集合之间的软匹配，再把这个匹配作为融合算子的一部分。它本质上是把 attention 里的“相似度归一化”升级为带全局约束的匹配问题：不只看局部 pairwise score，还考虑整体质量守恒或分配结构，因此能避免某些 token 被过度关注、另一些被系统性忽略。和普通 cross-attention 相比，这类方法的区别在于匹配矩阵不只是逐行 softmax，而是受 OT 约束的联合耦合；代价是计算更重、实现更复杂，通常需要 Sinkhorn 类近似来把复杂度压到可训练范围。是否值得用，取决于对齐难度是否真是瓶颈。","experiments":"从题目判断，实验重点应当是把 OT fusion 插到标准 Transformer 或多模态/多源任务中，和常规 attention-based fusion 比较精度、稳健性和可能的样本效率。这里没有提供任务、模型规模、数据集和具体数值，所以不能写具体提升幅度；合理预期的关键信息应包括：在输入对齐较难、信息冗余较高或 source mismatch 较强的设置下收益更明显，而在单源纯文本这类 attention 已足够强的场景，额外 OT 约束未必划算。最值得看的 ablation 会是 OT 正则强度、熵正则、Sinkhorn 迭代次数，以及和 cross-attention 直接替换还是并联融合的效果差异。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper addresses a structural weakness in standard Transformer fusion: most models merge representations through attention or simple weighting without explicitly solving the alignment problem between two sets of tokens. That works when alignment is easy, but becomes heuristic, data-hungry, and brittle when sources are heterogeneous or partially mismatched.","importance":"This is mainly a method and architecture contribution. Bringing optimal transport into Transformer fusion gives a different design lens: align first, then aggregate. That is relevant to multimodal pretraining, retrieval-augmented modeling, and cross-layer token fusion. The practical takeaway is not that OT always beats attention, but that explicit matching constraints can help when misalignment, redundancy, or domain shift is the real bottleneck.","technical_approach":"The core idea is to compute a soft transport plan between two sets of token representations and use that plan inside the fusion module. In contrast to standard cross-attention, which normalizes local pairwise scores row by row, OT solves a globally constrained coupling problem. That global structure can prevent a few tokens from absorbing most of the mass while others are ignored. The trade-off is cost: OT-style coupling is heavier and usually requires Sinkhorn-type approximations to remain trainable. So the design only pays off when alignment quality matters enough to justify the overhead.","experiments":"Given the title, the empirical section likely evaluates OT-based fusion against standard attention-based fusion across tasks that require combining multiple representation streams. The prompt does not provide datasets, model sizes, or numeric results, so no concrete gains should be invented. The key empirical questions are where OT helps: likely in settings with difficult alignment, high redundancy, or source mismatch, and less so in simple single-source text setups where standard attention is already strong. This question was mostly sidestepped for years because exact RTRL is prohibitively expensive, but interest has returned with online learning, long-sequence modeling, and recurrent architectures that try to avoid full-sequence backpropagation.","importance":"Its main value is in clarifying the operating regime of RTRL-style training rather than proposing an immediate replacement for mainstream optimization. The point is not a single trick, but a controlled study of where low-rank approximations, truncation, or unbiased estimators actually help. Relative to BPTT, RTRL offers true online updates and no need to store an entire unfolded sequence. The cost is substantial: maintaining gradient state is expensive, and approximations can inject enough noise to erase the theoretical advantage. The title already signals a balanced conclusion rather than a purely positive one.","experiments":"The most valuable experiments here are likely those that separate wall-clock speed, throughput, memory, latency, and convergence quality instead of only reporting final accuracy. 最大化时，不同样本或不同反馈的权重由置信度调节，而不是一视同仁地追奖励。这样的设计通常对应两层机制：一层估计奖励的不确定性或一致性，另一层在策略更新时对低置信奖励降权、截断或加入风险控制。与常规 reward finetuning 相比，它不是换一个更强 reward model，而是让优化过程知道 reward 何时不该被相信。有效性来自于减少错误高分样本对梯度的主导；代价是如果置信度估计本身偏差大，可能错过真正有价值但少见的样本。","experiments":"实验重点应当是在人类偏好、文本对齐度和图像质量之间拿到更好的 Pareto。合理的 baseline 包括普通 reward finetuning、偏好优化方法，以及可能的 classifier-free guidance 或 This matters beyond text-to-image: in both multimodal generation and LLM alignment, a large fraction of post-training failure comes from over-optimizing imperfect reward signals rather than from weak base models alone.","technical_approach":"The method is confidence-aware reward optimization. Instead of applying the same policy update to every reward signal, it estimates confidence or uncertainty in the reward and scales the update accordingly, for example by downweighting or constraining low-confidence feedback. This differs from standard reward finetuning, which usually only improves the reward model itself or changes the optimization algorithm without explicitly modeling trust in the reward. This differs from standard summarization, which optimizes coverage, and from passage reranking, which still leaves evidence fragmented. Post-hoc explanation methods are especially prone to reflecting superficial correlations.","importance":"This is a method-level contribution in interpretability and reliability, with a clear lesson for multimodal pretraining: if you want explanations to track internal evidence, you often need an architectural bottleneck that can be audited, not just visualization after the fact. It can also support dataset bias analysis and more controllable intermediate signals for alignment-stage critics/rewards.","technical_approach":"The core approach is a concept bottleneck model: predict a set of human-named concepts from the input, then predict the final output using only those concepts, so the explanation corresponds to the concept pathway. The mechanism is enforced information flow through the concept layer, typically trained with explicit concept supervision or auxiliary objectives. Prior fixes tweak advantage estimation or add regularization, but still do not explain which steps drive policy changes.","importance":"It is an interpretability- and stability-oriented contribution: decomposing policy updates to the step level to localize where updates come from under temporal correlation. For RLHF-style sequence decision problems, finer-grained updates may help control variance and mitigate reward hacking, at the cost of higher implementation and compute complexity.","technical_approach":"Step-based policy updates decompose episodic-return-driven learning into per-timestep contributions while explicitly accounting for correlations across adjacent steps, enabling inspection of how each step affects the policy gradient. Compared to trajectory-level updates, it is more diagnosable and amenable to targeted constraints. As a result, they lose badly to attention-based models in training throughput and often in practical relevance.","importance":"This matters at the algorithm-systems boundary. For pretraining, the main implication is that sequence models beyond Transformers may become more viable if nonlinear recurrences can be parallelized. It does not automatically beat attention, but it challenges the assumption that scalability requires either attention or strictly linear state evolution.","technical_approach":"From the title, the paper likely develops a general mechanism to parallelize nonlinear sequential updates across the length dimension. The hard part is to rewrite stepwise dependence into a composable or block-structured form that admits parallel prefix-style computation while retaining useful nonlinear expressivity. This differs from standard scan-based methods that mostly handle linear recurrences. The trade-off is usually some approximation error, additional memory, or restrictions on the class of nonlinear updates.","experiments":"No abstract or verified numbers are provided here. Exact speedups, model sizes, and accuracy deltas cannot be stated reliably.","authors_note":""},"tags":["sequence-parallelism","nonlinear-recurrence","parallel-algorithms","long-context","efficiency"]},{"id":"LinSQPG024","title":"Class Incremental Learning via Likelihood Ratio Based Task Prediction","authors":["Haowei Lin","Yijia Shao","Weinan Qian","Ningxin Pan","Yiduo Guo","Bing Liu"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"","venue":"ICLR","year":2024,"track":"main","award":"poster","links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/iclr/LinSQPG024","doi":""},"_dblp_key":"conf/iclr/LinSQPG024","_ee":"https://openreview.net/forum?id=8QfK9Dq4q0","category_id":"mechanism","relevance_score":6,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇工作处理的是类增量学习里的一个老问题：测试时样本属于哪个任务通常不知道，而任务识别一错，后续分类就会连带出错。过去很多 class-incremental 方法把任务预测当成附属步骤，依赖启发式分数或额外分类头，导致在类别不断增加时很不稳。","importance":"它的重要性主要限于 continual learning 子领域，对大模型预训练的直接关联较弱。对更广义生成建模的启发是：likelihood ratio 这类生成式判别信号在任务识别上可能比纯判别头更稳，但这更多是增量学习方法学上的价值，不是预训练范式层面的变化。","technical_approach":"从标题看，方法核心是用 likelihood ratio 来做任务预测，再服务于 class-incremental 学习。直觉上，作者不是直接比较各任务的原始似然，而是比较更稳健的似然比，这样可以减轻不同任务模型校准不一致、类数变化导致分数不可比的问题。和常见的基于最大 softmax 或最近原型的方法相比，这更像把任务识别转回生成式统计判别。边界在于：似然建模质量必须足够好，否则 ratio 也会继承偏差。","experiments":"当前信息不足以确认数据集与数值。通常这类论文会在 CIFAR-100、ImageNet 子集等 class-incremental 基准上，与 rehearsal、prompt-based、prototype-based 或 task-inference 方法比较平均增量准确率和 forgetting 指标，并做是否使用 ratio、如何估计似然的消融。具体结果这里不编造。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper focuses on a common failure point in class-incremental learning: task identity is unknown at test time, and errors in task prediction can dominate the final classification error. Many prior methods treat task inference as a side module with heuristic scores or extra heads, which becomes brittle as classes accumulate.","importance":"The contribution is mainly within continual learning and is only loosely connected to large-scale pretraining. The broader lesson is that generative signals such as likelihood ratios may offer more stable task inference than purely discriminative confidence scores, but this is a domain-specific methodological point rather than a shift in pretraining practice.","technical_approach":"From the title, the main idea is to use likelihood-ratio-based task prediction inside a class-incremental pipeline. The rationale is that raw likelihoods across tasks are often poorly calibrated and not directly comparable, whereas likelihood ratios can provide a more stable statistical decision signal. Compared with max-softmax or prototype-style task inference, this reframes the problem as generative discrimination. The limitation is obvious: if the underlying likelihood estimates are poor, the ratio inherits that weakness.","experiments":"The provided information does not include confirmed datasets or numbers. That has implications for data construction and augmentation design.","technical_approach":"From the title, the central move is spurious feature diversification rather than explicit suppression. Compared with invariant-risk or group-robust objectives, this is more of a data or representation intervention. The paper suggests that such intermediate signals need not be shaped only by end-task reward; representation-level objectives can make them easier to learn.","technical_approach":"The main idea is to regularize or train communication with a contrastive objective instead of relying purely on reinforcement learning. Messages are encouraged to be informative with respect to task-relevant states or intents by pulling positive pairs together and pushing negative pairs apart. That differs from classic emergent communication setups where messages are treated as black-box actions with no direct structure imposed on the message space. The likely benefit is better optimization and more semantically organized communication. The trade-off is that the method depends on sensible positive and negative pair construction, which may not transfer equally well across tasks.","experiments":"The expected experiments compare reward-only communication methods, differentiable communication baselines, and the proposed contrastive variant on standard multi-agent coordination environments. If tools like NTK and dataset distillation can explain when reconstruction is possible, they can guide more principled defense design.","technical_approach":"The technical angle is to analyze reconstruction attacks through the neural tangent kernel and dataset distillation. NTK offers a tractable approximation of training dynamics in wide networks, making it easier to reason about sample influence and local fitting, while dataset distillation provides a lens for whether a small synthetic proxy can stand in for the original data. Unlike attack-centric papers, this work is less about building a stronger reconstructor and more about building an explanatory framework. In practice, oscillation is often treated as synonymous with instability, which leads to conservative learning-rate choices that may slow training unnecessarily.","importance":"This is a theory paper, but it is highly relevant to large-scale pretraining. In real training runs, we constantly trade off throughput, stability, and final loss, and not every loss spike or parameter oscillation should be suppressed. A principled distinction between benign and harmful oscillation could justify more aggressive learning-rate schedules, shorter warmup, or different cooldown choices.","technical_approach":"The likely contribution is a theoretical analysis showing that under certain conditions, SGD can keep oscillating around a solution while maintaining good objective value or generalization. Unlike classical analyses that focus on monotone convergence, this line of work treats discrete-time optimization as a non-equilibrium process where oscillation can act like implicit regularization or help avoid sharp minima. If the paper proves formal conditions, they are likely stated for simplified settings with assumptions on smoothness, noise, or model class. The obvious limitation is that such results do not imply that all observed training spikes in large models are benign.","experiments":"The experiments likely move from controlled problems such as quadratics or linear models to neural networks, showing regimes where trajectories oscillate at large learning rates but performance remains good, and then identifying thresholds where the behavior becomes harmful. Relative to standard video diffusion transformers, the change is in the supervision pathway; relative to MAE-style masked modeling, the difference is that the goal remains generative rather than purely representational. That design makes sense because VLM outputs depend jointly on image features and prompt conditioning; an attack tied too tightly to one prompt can vanish under rewording. classification）”里，如何在长尾分布下同时做到高覆盖率（coverage）和低风险（risk），而不是只在头部类上看起来很安全。以往很多拒绝学习方法默认 i.i.d. 或者用单一阈值控制整体风险，结果往往把长尾样本系统性地拒掉或误判。","importance":"贡献更偏“方法+评估视角”：把 long-tail learning 的失败模式显式引入 reject option risk。对大模型预训练的启示是：当你用拒答、置信度或不确定性做安全策略时，数据分布的尾部会决定真实风险上界是否还成立。","technical_approach":"核心思路是把“拒绝”当成一种资源分配问题：在不同频次/难度的子群上分别校准拒绝策略，而不是用一个全局分数阈值一刀切。方法上通常会把风险约束从“总体”细化到“分组/类别/频次桶”，并分析在长尾下哪些校准或 surrogate policies can look safe on average while either rejecting most tail examples or making uncontrolled errors there. Prior work often assumes i.i.d. data and uses a single global threshold to trade coverage for risk, which is brittle when the tail dominates real-world risk.","importance":"This is mainly a method-and-evaluation contribution: it reframes rejection as something that must be analyzed per subgroup (e.g., head vs tail) rather than only via aggregate selective risk. Many existing pipelines rely on heuristics or strong distributional assumptions, so thresholds do not transfer across datasets.","importance":"This is a statistical-estimation contribution with direct operational impact: it turns the selective+OOD objective into estimable components and studies plug-in estimators for decision making. For pretrained-model deployment, the message is that ‘confidence threshold + OOD score’ often fails due to biased/unstable estimation and calibration, not necessarily due to the base model.","technical_approach":"The core approach rewrites target metrics as functions of conditional probabilities / risk terms / possibly density ratios, then replaces unknown terms with consistent plug-in estimates and analyzes how estimation error propagates to the final selective decision. Guttag 和 Adrian V. Earlier work mostly focused on capacity and conditioning design, but the parameterization itself strongly shapes optimization dynamics, and naive parameterizations can make learning scale-sensitive in a bad way.","importance":"This is a method-level optimization contribution rather than a paradigm shift. Its direct impact on pretraining is limited, but it is relevant anywhere a network generates weights for another network—meta-learning, conditional adaptation, dynamic layers, and some efficient fine-tuning setups. If the claimed stability gains hold broadly, the idea is worth carrying into larger conditional-weight systems.","technical_approach":"The core proposal is a magnitude-invariant parameterization that reduces sensitivity to overall weight scaling. Under standard parameterizations, small changes in output magnitude from the hypernetwork can distort both the represented function and the gradient scale, so optimization chases amplitude effects instead of structure; the proposed design tries to separate directional learning from raw magnitude, making gradients more comparable across layers and conditioning inputs. This differs from standard normalization because it modifies how weights are generated, not just how activations are normalized afterward.","experiments":"The experiments likely show faster convergence, better final performance, or reduced tuning sensitivity across several hypernetwork settings. Kaplan"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"","venue":"ICLR","year":2024,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/iclr/OsmanK24","doi":""},"_dblp_key":"conf/iclr/OsmanK24","_ee":"https://openreview.net/forum?id=owfuhF0bT9","category_id":"multimodal_gen","relevance_score":6,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇论文要解决的是：扩散模型里的 Transformer 去噪器成本太高，能否通过参数共享把模型做小很多，同时尽量不牺牲生成质量。以往扩散模型通常沿用每层独立参数的标准 Transformer，这在图像建模里效果好但训练和部署都很重；如果时间步之间和层之间存在大量重复计算结构，那么参数共享可能是一个被低估的效率杠杆。","importance":"这项工作属于系统与架构效率层面的改进。它对 pretrain 的启示在于，扩散式生成模型未必需要按 LLM 的“层层堆独立块”习惯扩展，某些任务上重复迭代结构本来就更适合共享参数；如果能在接近质量不变的情况下大幅减参，就会影响多模态预训练里 AR+diffusion 联合建模的算力分配方式。","technical_approach":"核心方法是把参数共享系统性地引入扩散 Transformer，而不是零散地做轻量化技巧。直觉上，扩散去噪在不同深度和不同时间步执行的是高度相似的变换，因此完全独立的层参数存在冗余；作者应当探索跨层共享、重复 block、可能配合少量时间条件调制或层特定缩放，以保留表达力。和直接缩小 hidden size 或层数相比，共享参数的好处是能保留较深的计算图和迭代推理深度，代价则是容量上限可能更早受限。","experiments":"实验的关键事实应围绕参数量—质量权衡。论文大概率报告在图像扩散任务上，用更少参数达到接近 baseline 的 FID 或 likelihood，并比较与单纯小模型的差异；如果共享设计有效，它应该在同等参数预算下优于 naïve shrink。额外有价值的 ablation 会包括共享粒度、是否需要层特定调制，以及共享对训练稳定性的影响。由于没有摘要与表格，无法安全给出数据集、参数规模和具体分数。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper addresses a practical bottleneck: diffusion Transformers are expensive, so can we share parameters aggressively and still keep most of the generation quality? Standard diffusion models usually keep separate parameters per layer, which is effective but costly; if denoising repeatedly applies similar transformations, that design may be over-parameterized.","importance":"This is primarily an efficiency and architecture paper. The pretraining relevance is that diffusion-style generators may not need to scale in the same fully independent-layer way as autoregressive LMs; if parameter sharing preserves quality at much lower parameter count, it changes the compute allocation story for multimodal pretraining and AR+diffusion hybrids.","technical_approach":"The main idea is to introduce systematic parameter sharing into diffusion Transformers rather than relying on ad hoc model shrinking. The intuition is that denoising across depth and timesteps uses highly repetitive computation, so fully distinct layer weights are redundant; the design likely shares blocks across layers while keeping a small amount of timestep conditioning or layer-specific modulation to preserve flexibility. Compared with simply reducing width or depth, sharing keeps a deeper computation graph but may cap representational capacity sooner.","experiments":"The key experimental question is the parameter-quality trade-off. In high-resolution regimes, UNet-heavy latent diffusion and multi-step sampling become the dominant bottlenecks.","importance":"This is an architecture-level contribution: Würstchen proposes a more efficient diffusion design that decomposes high-resolution generation into stages rather than pushing all work through an expensive UNet diffusion at full resolution. VLM 在回答视觉问题时经常抓不住问题里的视觉落点，导致模型会语言上“会答题”，但没有真正把问题和图像区域对齐。过去很多方法默认问题本身已经足够清晰，或只在答案端做推理增强，较少显式改写问题并补充视觉 grounding 信号，因此在复杂指代、多跳属性和细粒度定位上容易出错。","importance":"这项工作的价值主要在方法层和评测层：它把“问题重写 + 视觉落点显式化 + chain-of-thought 或直接 region prompting 的区别在于，这里把“问法”本身当成可优化对象，目标是减少模型在第一步就找错视觉实体的概率。它的有效性来自把语言歧义前移处理：如果问题先被规范化，后续视觉检索和答案生成的误差会更小。代价是流程更长，也更依赖重写模块的质量；若重写偏离原意，错误会被放大。","experiments":"从给定信息里无法确认具体实验数字、模型规模、数据规模和提升幅度。可以确认的只有：论文关注视觉问答中的 grounding 与 reasoning，合理的关键对比应包括原始 VLM、仅重写、仅增强、以及完整 RAR 流程，并检验在需要细粒度定位或多步推理的数据集上是否更稳。若实验设计完善，最有价值的消融通常会回答两件事：一是收益主要来自问题重写还是视觉增强；二是该方法对强闭源/开源 VLM 是否都稳定有效。","authors_note":"UNC Chapel Hill。Mohit Bansal surface of a question without grounding the question to the right visual evidence. Prior work mostly strengthened answer generation or reasoning, while treating the question itself as fixed, which leaves ambiguity, reference resolution, and visual alignment under-addressed.","importance":"The contribution is mainly methodological rather than paradigm-shifting. It argues that VLM errors are not only about weak visual encoders or weak decoders; question formulation is itself a bottleneck. For pretraining-oriented readers, the useful takeaway is that alignment can be improved not only at the image-text pair level but also at the task/query level, although this paper is closer to inference-time augmentation than to a new pretraining objective.","technical_approach":"The method is a question-centric pipeline rather than a new VLM architecture. It first rephrases the question to make references and constraints more explicit, then augments it with visually relevant cues such as candidate regions or intermediate descriptions, and finally asks the model to reason over these grounded anchors. The key design choice is to optimize the question representation before reasoning, instead of relying on raw prompting or pure chain-of-thought. This can work because many downstream failures start with misidentifying the target entity. The trade-off is straightforward: the pipeline is longer and becomes sensitive to rephrasing quality; a bad rewrite can amplify the error.","experiments":"The provided information does not include verified numbers for model size, data scale, datasets, or gains. What can be inferred is that the paper likely compares a base VLM, rephrase-only, augment-only, and the full pipeline on visual question answering settings that require precise grounding and multi-step reasoning. That matters because many embodied failures come from dynamic affordances and social constraints rather than map understanding alone.
