3D 物体时，纯 2D diffusion prior 往往多视角不一致，纯 3D prior 又容易细节不足、优化困难。过去很多方法在 2D 质量和 3D 一致性之间二选一，因此生成结果要么好看但转一圈就崩，要么几何稳定但纹理粗糙。","importance":"这项工作的意义主要在 3D 生成方法层，不是典型语言预训练工作。但它对统一生成范式有启发，因为它展示了如何把 2D 和 3D 两类先验放在一个优化链条里协同使用，而不是让其中一方主导。对关注 AR+diffusion 或跨模态统一建模的人，这类工作有参考价值：不同模态/表示空间的 prior 可以承担不同职责，关键是接口设计。","technical_approach":"核心方法是从单张图出发，联合利用 2D diffusion prior 和 3D diffusion prior 来生成 3D 物体。常见思路是先用 2D prior 提供高保真外观约束，再用 3D prior 提供几何与多视角一致性约束，二者在优化阶段分工。与只靠 SDS 类 2D score distillation 的方法相比，这里增加了显式 3D 先验，目的是减少 Janus 问题和视角漂移；与只靠 3D 生成器的方法相比，它又保留了来自强 2D 图像模型的纹理细节。这样设计有效，是因为 2D 模型和 3D 模型擅长的统计结构不同。代价在于训练/优化链更复杂，也更依赖两个 prior 的兼容性和权重平衡。","experiments":"从给定信息里无法确认所用 2D/3D backbone、数据集、几何指标或用户研究结果。合理的实验应比较仅 2D prior、仅 3D prior 和联合 prior 的版本，并同时报告外观质量与 3D 一致性，而不是只放渲染图。最有价值的证据应是：联合方法在单图重建或新视角合成上明显减少多头/双面等 Janus artifact，同时保持更好的纹理保真度。若论文完整，通常还会有不同优化阶段或 loss 权重的消融，说明两个 prior 的职责分工。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper addresses a common failure mode in single-image 3D generation: 2D diffusion priors give strong appearance but weak multi-view consistency, while 3D priors improve geometry but often lose detail or are harder to optimize. Earlier methods usually favored one side, producing either attractive but unstable renderings or consistent but bland 3D shapes.","importance":"This is primarily a 3D generation method paper, not a core language pretraining paper. Still, it is relevant at the level of unified generative modeling because it shows how two different priors, defined in 2D and 3D spaces, can be combined in one optimization pipeline with complementary roles. That is a useful design pattern for broader multimodal modeling.","technical_approach":"The main idea is to generate a 3D object from one image using both a 2D diffusion prior and a 3D diffusion prior. In this kind of design, the 2D prior typically provides high-fidelity appearance supervision, while the 3D prior regularizes geometry and cross-view consistency. Compared with methods that rely only on 2D score distillation, adding an explicit 3D prior should reduce Janus artifacts and view drift. Compared with pure 3D generation, it preserves detail from strong image models. The design works because the two priors capture different statistical structure. The trade-off is a more complex optimization stack and sensitivity to how the two priors are balanced.","experiments":"The provided information does not include verified details about the 2D/3D backbones, datasets, or quantitative metrics. A convincing evaluation should compare 2D-only, 3D-only, and combined-prior variants, and report both visual fidelity and 3D consistency rather than qualitative images alone. The most meaningful evidence would be reduced Janus-style artifacts under novel-view rendering while preserving finer texture details. Good ablations would clarify the respective roles of the two priors and the effect of stage-wise optimization or loss weighting.","authors_note":""},"tags":["diffusion","3d-generation","multimodal","single-image"],"notable_authors":["Guocheng Qian","Jinjie Mai","Abdullah Hamdi","Jian Ren","Aliaksandr prompting，它舍弃了全局可见数据，换来隐私保留；相比纯本地 prompt，它又通过联邦聚合提高泛化。这样设计有效，是因为 prompt 空间相对低维，适合作为联邦通信对象。边界则是：客户端分布差异越大，单一全局 prompt 越可能失效，因此常需要个性化或分层聚合。","experiments":"从给定信息里无法确认具体 VLM prompt，以及在客户端异质性增强时方法是否更稳。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper studies prompt generation for vision-language models in a federated setting, where data are distributed across clients, text distributions are heterogeneous, and privacy constraints prevent centralized training. Many prompt learning methods implicitly assume global data access, which makes them a poor fit for federated deployment.","importance":"This is an intersection of federated learning and VLM adaptation, so it is somewhat downstream from core pretraining. Still, it is practically relevant for distributed post-training. The useful point is that prompts are not automatically federated-friendly just because they are lightweight; text-driven prompt generation can break under client heterogeneity unless aggregation and personalization are designed carefully.","technical_approach":"The likely method is federated text-driven prompt generation: each client generates or updates prompts from local textual or vision-language signals, and the server aggregates them into a shared or partially personalized prompt representation. Unlike standard FedAvg over model parameters, prompt generation here depends on semantic structure in text, so the main challenge is aligning heterogeneous client-specific prompts and reducing client drift. Compared with centralized text-driven prompting, the method gives up full-data visibility for privacy. Compared with purely local prompts, it gains generalization through federation. This can work because the prompt space is relatively small and communication-efficient. The limitation is clear: as client distributions diverge, a single global prompt becomes less adequate, so some form of personalization is often necessary.","experiments":"The provided information does not include verified VLM backbones, number of clients, datasets, or exact gaps to centralized methods. Blocher"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"","venue":"ICLR","year":2024,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/iclr/RodemannB24","doi":""},"_dblp_key":"conf/iclr/RodemannB24","_ee":"https://openreview.net/forum?id=mhwNoQcEjQ","category_id":"mechanism","relevance_score":6,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇论文要解决的是：在优化器比较中，很多结论依赖“全序排名”（谁第一谁第二），但在噪声、超参敏感和任务差异下，全序往往不稳且误导；作者提出用“部分排序”来表达哪些优化器在统计上可区分、哪些其实等价。过去常见做法是报告单点最优或平均排名，但这会把不显著的差异硬排出先后。","importance":"贡献是方法论层面的：给优化器评测一个更稳健的统计表达方式，减少“优化器论文靠挑任务/挑超参赢”的空间。对大规模 pretrain 的启示是：当差异小于训练噪声或复现实验方差时，应该承认不可区分，并把精力放在稳定性、成本与可调性上，而不是追逐脆弱的 SOTA 排名。","technical_approach":"核心做法是把优化器比较从“输出一个排名”改为“输出一个偏序关系图”：只有当 A 在多个设置下显著优于 B 时才建立 A≻B，否则保持不可比或并列。关键机制通常依赖成对统计检验/置信区间与多重比较校正，并把超参搜索预算显式纳入比较框架；代价是结果更难用一句话营销，但更接近工程现实。","experiments":"未提供摘要，无法给出覆盖的优化器集合（AdamW/SGD/Lion 等）、任务（CV/NLP/LM）、训练预算与显著性阈值等细节。合理的关键实验应展示：全序排名在重复实验/换任务后不稳定，而部分排序能稳定识别“确定更差/确定更好/无法区分”的集合，并分析超参预算变化对偏序结构的影响，但需以论文为准。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper addresses a common failure mode in optimizer benchmarking: forcing a total order ranking is often unstable under noise, hyperparameter sensitivity, and task variation. It argues for partial rankings that only assert orderings when differences are statistically distinguishable.","importance":"This is a methodology contribution that makes optimizer evaluation harder to game and more reproducible. For large-scale pretraining, it encourages treating many “wins” as indistinguishable within variance, shifting attention to stability, cost, and tunability rather than fragile leaderboard-style rankings.","technical_approach":"The approach replaces a single ranked list with a poset: declare A≻B only when evidence across settings supports a significant advantage; otherwise keep ties or incomparability. token 层而不是只在下游任务里补救。以往很多 protein LM 主要沿用文本 LM 的离散词表，把三维结构当额外监督或后融合特征；SaProt 认为这样会错过最关键的一步——表示单元本身没有把结构约束编码进去。","importance":"这项工作的贡献更偏表示与 tokenizer 设计，虽然对象是蛋白而不是自然语言，但对 pretrain 的方法核心是结构感知词表，即把蛋白序列与局部结构状态联合编码成离散 token，再做语言建模。关键设计选择不是把结构作为额外 side information 拼给 encoder，而是把“残基是什么”和“它处在怎样的结构环境里”一起变成建模基本单位；这样自监督目标在每一步预测时都被迫同时考虑序列和结构依赖。和标准 amino-acid tokenizer 相比，这种做法提高了单 token 信息密度，但代价是词表构造更依赖高质量结构注释，泛化到结构缺失数据时也更考验预处理策略。","experiments":"给定信息没有提供可核实的参数量、训练数据规模和具体指标，因此不应虚构。可以比较确定的是，论文应在蛋白理解与设计相关 benchmark 上对比标准 protein LM，并通过 ablation 验证结构感知词表优于仅加结构特征或仅用序列词表。对 pretrain 读者更值得关注的是：收益来自更好的 tokenization 还是来自额外结构监督，这个拆分会决定方法是否能迁移到其他模态。","authors_note":"机构与团队细节无法从给定内容可靠确认。"},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper tackles a simple but important limitation in protein language modeling: sequence-only tokens discard structure too early. Many protein LMs inherit the text-LM recipe and try to add structural information later through auxiliary supervision or downstream heads. SaProt argues that this is suboptimal because the modeling unit itself should already encode structural context.","importance":"This is primarily a representation and tokenizer contribution, but it is relevant well beyond proteins. The lesson for pretraining is that the vocabulary is not a neutral interface: token design determines which inductive biases the model can absorb efficiently. That is the same design problem we face in multimodal pretraining when deciding how to discretize images, audio, or other structured signals.","technical_approach":"SaProt introduces a structure-aware vocabulary that jointly represents amino-acid identity and structural state, then trains a language model over those tokens. The key choice is to bake structure into the basic prediction unit instead of treating it as optional side information. That raises the information content of each token and forces the objective to model sequence and structure jointly. The trade-off is that the tokenizer depends more heavily on reliable structural annotations and preprocessing.","experiments":"The provided metadata does not include verifiable parameter counts, training scale, or exact benchmark numbers, so those should be left unspecified. What is most likely shown is that a structure-aware vocabulary outperforms sequence-only tokenization across protein understanding or design tasks, with ablations separating the effect of token design from simply adding more supervision. For pretraining readers, that decomposition is the most important part.","authors_note":""},"tags":["tokenizer","protein-language-model","structure-aware","pretraining","data-efficiency"]},{"id":"SzotSAMMTMHT24","title":"Large Language Models as Generalizable Policies for Embodied Tasks","authors":["Andrew Szot","Max Schwarzer","Harsh Agrawal","Bogdan Mazoure","Rin Metcalf","Walter Talbott","Natalie Mackraz","R. Practitioners default to momentum, but its marginal benefit in the small-LR regime is theoretically unclear.","importance":"A theoretical contribution with practical implications for optimizer tuning. Relevant for pretraining decisions about using synthetic data (e.g., synthetic captions, generated code).","technical_approach":"Analyzes from both theoretical and experimental angles. Compared to LLMs or GANs, diffusion’s long sampling chain and continuous outputs make “reproduction” harder to define and measure, which has slowed systematic privacy evaluation.","importance":"This is a safety/evaluation-method contribution with direct deployment implications. For pretraining, it highlights that privacy risk in diffusion is shaped not only by dataset size but also by denoising training dynamics and sampling procedures; mitigation will typically trade off fidelity/diversity, so the useful output is a clear trade-off curve rather than a single headline number.","technical_approach":"The approach typically has three components: (1) detection via nearest-neighbor retrieval, reconstruction similarity, membership inference, or feature-space matching; (2) explanation by correlating memorization with sample frequency/difficulty, noise levels, or gradient contributions across training; and (3) mitigation via dedup/augmentation, regularization, differentially private training, or sampling-time anti-replication heuristics. Many samplers optimize speed–quality but do not explicitly address numerical-instability-style artifacts.","importance":"This is a sampling-algorithm contribution: it imports momentum-like updates from optimization to smooth sampling dynamics and reduce oscillation/divergence. For generative pretraining, it reinforces that some quality issues are sampler-dynamics-limited rather than model-capacity-limited, so improving the sampler can be cheaper than retraining. The boundary is extra hyperparameters and a potential stability–sharpness trade-off.","technical_approach":"The method introduces a momentum state into each denoising update, combining current predictions with an accumulated velocity (e.g., exponential moving average) to damp high-frequency oscillations. Key choices are: (1) scheduling the momentum coefficient across timesteps to avoid over-smoothing early noisy stages, (2) coupling with classifier-free guidance to prevent bias accumulation at high CFG, and (3) plug-and-play compatibility with existing samplers. Unlike post-hoc output smoothing, it modifies the sampling dynamics to behave like a more stable numerical integrator.","experiments":"Without the abstract, datasets, step budgets, and FID/quality numbers cannot be reported. Prior work mostly leaned on larger models, more parallel data, or black-box reranking, which improves averages but does not explain failure modes or support controllable repair.","importance":"This is mainly a methods-and-systems contribution rather than a pretraining-level one. Its relevance to pretraining is indirect but useful: when scaling the base model is expensive, an interpretable post-generation correction layer can recover quality cheaply, especially for code where syntax and semantics impose strong structure.","technical_approach":"The likely contribution is an interpretable post-translation correction pipeline instead of a new translation model. The design intuition is to decompose translation mistakes into identifiable error types and then apply targeted edits, constraints, or repair rules. This differs from regenerate-or-rerank approaches because it preserves correct parts of the initial translation, reduces search space, and can justify why each fix was made. That design is well matched to code translation, where many errors are local: API mapping mismatches, typing inconsistencies, syntax glitches, and control-flow details. The trade-off is clear: once the translation is semantically off at a higher level, a repair layer has limited headroom.","experiments":"The key experimental evidence should be gains on code translation benchmarks in metrics such as exact match, compilability, or unit-test pass rate, plus breakdowns by error category. The strongest case would be not just a better aggregate score, but showing that the method consistently fixes common local errors, is more stable and auditable than black-box post-processing, and has identifiable failure cases on semantic mistranslations or long-range dependencies. No abstract or numeric results were provided, so model size, data scale, baselines, and effect sizes cannot be stated reliably.","authors_note":""},"tags":["code translation","program repair","interpretability","code generation","verification"]},{"id":"XueJNM24","title":"Understanding the Robustness of Multi-modal Contrastive Learning to Distribution Shift","authors":["Yihao Xue","Siddharth Joshi","Dang Nguyen","Baharan Mirzasoleiman"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"","venue":"ICLR","year":2024,"track":"main","award":"poster","links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/iclr/XueJNM24","doi":""},"_dblp_key":"conf/iclr/XueJNM24","_ee":"https://openreview.net/forum?id=rtl4XnJYBh","category_id":"multimodal","relevance_score":6,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇论文研究的是 multi-modal contrastive learning 在分布偏移下到底稳不稳，以及稳健性来自哪里。过去很多图文对比学习工作主要报告 in-distribution 检索或零样本分类结果，对真实部署中常见的模态偏移、语义偏移和相关性断裂分析不够细。","importance":"这项工作对多模态预训练是有直接价值的，因为它不只是测一个新 benchmark，而是在拆解 CLIP 类目标函数的鲁棒性边界。对 pretrain 的启示是：对比学习学到的对齐未必等于可迁移语义，模型在分布偏移下到底依赖语义一致性、背景共现还是模态捷径，需要更细的诊断；这会影响数据配比、负样本构造和后续对齐目标设计。","technical_approach":"核心方法应当是系统性地构造或分析多种 distribution shift，并比较 multi-modal contrastive learning 的表示退化模式。与单纯报告 OOD 准确率不同，这类工作通常会分离不同 shift 来源，例如视觉外观变化、文本描述风格变化、标签先验变化或跨模态相关性变化，再观察表示空间、相似度打分和检索/分类行为如何变化。这样的设计有效，因为对比学习目标只要求正样本更近、负样本更远，它可能同时学习到稳健语义和脆弱捷径；只有把 shift 类型拆开，才能看出模型到底在利用什么。","experiments":"实验的关键事实应当包括：在若干多模态基准上，对比学习模型在不同 shift 条件下的性能下降曲线，以及和监督式、多任务式或生成式视觉语言模型的比较。真正有价值的结果通常不是“总体更鲁棒”或“总体更脆弱”，而是不同 shift 下表现不一致：例如对单模态噪声较稳，但对跨模态相关性破坏更敏感；或者检索比分类更稳。若论文做得扎实，还会给出 embedding geometry、temperature、负样本规模等因素的 ablation。由于缺少摘要与数值，无法安全填写具体数据集、模型规模和提升数字。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"This paper asks a concrete question: how robust is multimodal contrastive learning under distribution shift, and what kind of robustness does it actually learn? Much of the prior literature focused on in-distribution retrieval or zero-shot classification, leaving the failure modes under real deployment shifts underexplored.","importance":"The contribution matters directly for multimodal pretraining because it probes the limits of CLIP-style objectives rather than introducing yet another benchmark score. The pretraining takeaway is that alignment learned by contrastive loss is not automatically the same as transferable semantics. Under shift, the model may rely on semantics, co-occurrence statistics, or cross-modal shortcuts, and that distinction should influence data mixture, negative construction, and alignment objectives.","technical_approach":"The likely approach is a systematic study of different shift types and how contrastive representations degrade under each. This is more informative than reporting a single OOD accuracy number. A careful setup would separate image-side appearance shifts, text-style shifts, label prior shifts, and broken cross-modal correlations, then inspect how embedding geometry, similarity scores, retrieval, and classification respond. That design is appropriate because contrastive learning only enforces relative proximity; it can reward both robust semantics and brittle shortcuts. Disentangling shift sources is the only way to see which one the model is using.","experiments":"The key results should include performance drops across multiple multimodal benchmarks and shift settings, ideally compared against supervised, multitask, or generative vision-language baselines. The most useful outcome would be a non-uniform robustness profile rather than a single winner: for example, robustness to unimodal corruption but sensitivity to cross-modal correlation breakage, or stronger retrieval robustness than classification robustness. Strong ablations would examine temperature, negative pool size, and embedding geometry. Instead, the method probably uses dynamic weighting, selective adaptation, or constraints that prevent a temporarily dominant modality from driving all parameter updates. This makes sense because multimodal corruption is often asymmetric: one modality may be noisy, missing, or shifted while another remains informative. Baldridge","Jiahui Yu"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"","venue":"ICLR","year":2024,"track":"main","award":"poster","links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/iclr/YouGWCBY24","doi":""},"_dblp_key":"conf/iclr/YouGWCBY24","_ee":"https://openreview.net/forum?id=8ISRqgtjPc","category_id":"multimodal_gen","relevance_score":6,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇论文要解决的是：图文生成模型往往是单向的（文生图或图生文）且训练目标与对齐目标割裂，导致生成质量与跨模态一致性难同时优化。以往常见做法是 CLIP 式对比对齐 + 单向生成器，或者分别训练两个方向。","importance":"贡献偏统一建模方法级：提出对比学习 + 双向生成的联合框架（contrastive bi-directional image-text generation），更接近“一个模型同时会描述与会生成”。对跨模态 pretrain 的启示是：对比损失提供全局对齐，生成损失提供细粒度条件建模，两者联合能减少只对齐不生成或只生成不对齐的偏差；代价是训练更重、目标权重更敏感。","technical_approach":"CoBIT 的核心是一个双向图文生成模型，并引入对比目标来约束共享表征空间，使得图→文与文→图共享一致的语义锚点。关键设计取舍是把“生成”与“对齐”放在同一训练循环里，而不是先对齐再微调生成；这通常能提升一致性，但也可能出现对比目标压制生成多样性，需要在温度/权重/采样策略上平衡。","experiments":"未提供摘要与实验细节，无法给出使用的图像 tokenizer（VQ/离散码本或连续扩散）、模型规模、训练数据（如 LAION 规模）、以及在 COCO caption、text-to-image FID/CLIPScore 等指标上的具体提升。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper addresses a mismatch in vision-language generation: many systems are one-directional (image→text or text→image) and optimize alignment and generation separately, making it hard to jointly improve fidelity and cross-modal consistency. Common pipelines pair CLIP-style contrastive alignment with a separate generator, or train two directions independently.","importance":"This is a unified modeling contribution: a contrastive + bidirectional generation framework that pushes toward a single model that can both describe and generate. For multimodal pretraining, the key insight is that contrastive loss provides global semantic alignment while generative loss enforces fine-grained conditional modeling; combining them can reduce “aligned but not generative” or “generative but misaligned” failure modes, at the cost of heavier training and sensitive loss balancing.","technical_approach":"CoBIT trains a bidirectional image-text generator and adds a contrastive objective to anchor a shared representation space so both directions stay semantically consistent. Baldridge"]},{"id":"Yuan0L24","title":"Early Stopping Against Label Noise Without Validation Data","authors":["Suqin Yuan","Lei Feng","Tongliang Liu"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"","venue":"ICLR","year":2024,"track":"main","award":"poster","links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/iclr/Yuan0L24","doi":""},"_dblp_key":"conf/iclr/Yuan0L24","_ee":"https://openreview.net/forum?id=CMzF2aOfqp","category_id":"mechanism","relevance_score":6,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇工作解决的是带噪标签训练里的一个硬问题：没有干净验证集时，如何判断该在什么时候 early stopping。传统早停依赖验证集监控泛化误差，但在弱监督、网页数据或自动标注场景里，验证集往往也不干净或根本没有，因此训练很容易在后期记住噪声标签。","importance":"它属于训练方法层面的实用工作。对 pretrain 虽然不是直接相关，但对大规模弱监督或合成标签数据训练有借鉴意义：当监督信号质量不稳定时，训练动态本身可以被当作噪声识别信号，帮助决定何时停止、何时切换阶段，这和 pretrain / mid-train 的 cooldown、data annealing 思路是相通的。","technical_approach":"方法核心应是从训练过程内部提取一个无需验证集的停止准则，利用模型先学简单和干净样本、后拟合噪声样本的动态差异。常见可行路径包括监控损失分布、预测一致性、样本置信度演化或梯度统计，并把某个转折点作为最佳停止时刻。它与直接估计噪声率的方法不同，更像是把 memorization onset 作为可观测事件。这样设计有效，因为噪声学习通常发生在训练后段且伴随表示或置信度结构变化。边界在于，当噪声非常结构化、样本极难或模型容量偏小，这种“先干净后噪声”的分离未必明显。","experiments":"从题目看，实验应覆盖不同噪声类型和噪声率下的分类任务，并对比使用验证集早停、固定 epoch 训练和若干无验证集基线。给定信息中没有摘要，因此无法可靠写出数据集、网络结构和具体百分点提升。比较值得关注的结果通常是：在中高噪声下是否接近 oracle early stopping；在对称噪声和实例相关噪声上的表现差异；以及是否对模型架构和优化器选择稳定。若这些点成立，它会是一篇偏稳健训练而非 LLM 核心方法的论文。","authors_note":"Lei Feng、Tongliang Liu 是噪声学习方向的知名作者。具体机构无法从给定信息确认。"},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper tackles a hard but practical problem in learning with label noise: how to early-stop without access to a clean validation set. Standard early stopping assumes a reliable validation signal, but in weakly supervised, web-scale, or auto-labeled settings that assumption often fails, and prolonged training ends up memorizing noisy labels.","importance":"This is a useful training-method contribution. It is not directly about LLM pretraining, but the lesson transfers to large-scale weak supervision: when the supervision signal is unreliable, the training dynamics themselves may reveal when learning starts to overfit noise. That connects naturally to ideas like cooldown, stage transitions, and data annealing in broader pretraining pipelines.","technical_approach":"The core idea is likely to derive a stopping criterion from the training trajectory itself, exploiting the well-known dynamics where models fit easier and cleaner samples before memorizing noisy ones. Practical signals might include loss-shape changes, confidence evolution, prediction consistency, or gradient statistics, with the stop point tied to a detectable transition. Unlike methods that first estimate the noise rate, this framing treats the onset of memorization as the observable event. The approach is sensible because noisy-label overfitting tends to emerge later in training and often leaves signatures in representation or confidence dynamics. Its boundary is that the clean-first, noisy-later separation can weaken when the noise is highly structured, the task is intrinsically hard, or model capacity is limited.","experiments":"The experiments should cover multiple noise regimes and compare the proposed criterion against oracle validation-based stopping, fixed-epoch training, and prior no-validation baselines. The provided metadata lacks the abstract, so I cannot reliably report datasets, architectures, or exact gains. What would matter most is whether the method tracks oracle stopping under medium-to-high noise, how it behaves under symmetric versus instance-dependent noise, and whether it is stable across model and optimizer choices. If those hold, this is best viewed as a robust-training paper with indirect relevance to large-scale weak supervision.","authors_note":"Lei Feng and Tongliang Liu are well-known researchers in learning with noisy labels. Earlier methods often optimized readability or agreement with annotated rationales, but did not explicitly deal with shortcuts, meaning the model could rely on superficial cues while generating polished post-hoc explanations.","importance":"This is mainly a paper about interpretability and signal quality. Its relevance to pretraining and post-training is that once a model is allowed to exploit shortcuts, any later explanation is likely to become a justification rather than a faithful account. Improving explanation faithfulness therefore often requires first discovering and suppressing shortcut behavior, which is closely related to reward hacking and spurious chain-of-thought issues.","technical_approach":"The key idea is to boost rationalization by discovering shortcuts and then using that information to reshape training or constrain explanation generation. Compared with methods that only supervise rationales, this adds a more causal intervention: if the model is found to depend on spurious but correlated patterns, training can penalize, mask, or reweight those patterns so that both prediction and explanation move toward more genuine evidence. This design is sensible because unfaithful explanations often originate upstream in a shortcut-driven predictor, not in the explanation generator itself. The limitation is that shortcut discovery is never complete, and when causal and correlated features are tightly entangled, removing shortcuts can also hurt task accuracy.","experiments":"Such papers usually evaluate task accuracy together with rationale plausibility and faithfulness, often on text classification or NLU benchmarks, and compare against rationale-supervision baselines. The provided metadata lacks the abstract, so I cannot reliably state datasets, models, or exact gains. The most important empirical questions are whether shortcut discovery makes explanations better support the model’s prediction, whether robustness improves on counterfactual or out-of-distribution examples, and how much task accuracy is traded off for better faithfulness. If those points are well supported, the contribution is less about generating prettier explanations and more about reframing explanation quality as a shortcut-learning problem.","authors_note":""},"tags":["interpretability","faithfulness","rationalization","shortcuts","evaluation"]},{"id":"YvinecDB24","title":"Network Memory Footprint Compression Through Jointly Learnable Codebooks and Mappings","authors":["Edouard Yvinec","Arnaud Dapogny","Kevin Bailly"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"","venue":"ICLR","year":2024,"track":"main","award":"poster","links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/iclr/YvinecDB24","doi":""},"_dblp_key":"conf/iclr/YvinecDB24","_ee":"https://openreview.net/forum?id=1RrOtCmuKr","category_id":"efficient","relevance_score":6,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{},"summary_en":{},"tags":["model-compression","codebook","vector-quantization","memory-footprint"]},{"id":"ZangGS024","title":"Overcoming the Pitfalls of Vision-Language Model Finetuning for OOD Generalization","authors":["Yuhang Zang","Hanlin Goh","Joshua M. In large models and long-context settings, memory often becomes the limiting factor before raw compute does. Existing solutions tend to rely on training-oriented checkpointing, hand-crafted chunking, or operator-specific optimizations, which are either non-automatic or architecture-dependent.","importance":"This is a systems contribution with direct practical value for both deployment and research iteration. From a pretraining perspective, it is a reminder that many apparent model-size limits are actually memory-scheduling limits. Better activation planning at inference can enable larger batches, longer contexts, or cheaper evaluation on the same hardware.","technical_approach":"AutoChunk appears to automatically derive activation chunking strategies for memory-efficient inference. Unlike fixed heuristics or manually chosen split points, it likely analyzes the computation graph, tensor dependencies, and peak-memory regions to decide where and how much to chunk. The design logic is straightforward: not all intermediate activations need to reside in memory at the same time, and careful scheduling can trade a small amount of recomputation or execution fragmentation for a much lower memory peak. The boundary is also clear: overly fine chunking increases launch overhead, synchronization cost, and latency, so this is fundamentally a memory-versus-throughput trade-off.","experiments":"For a paper like this, the key experiments are memory reduction, throughput and latency impact, and generalization across architectures and sequence lengths. Gower","Robin Walters","Rose Yu"],"institutions":["UC San Diego","Northeastern University"],"abstract":"","venue":"ICLR","year":2024,"track":"main","award":"oral","links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/iclr/ZhaoG0Y24","doi":""},"_dblp_key":"conf/iclr/ZhaoG0Y24","_ee":"https://openreview.net/forum?id=L0r0GphlIL","category_id":"mechanism","relevance_score":6,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"神经网络参数空间存在对称性（如 permutation symmetry），导致损失面上有大量等价点，但现有优化器完全忽略这些结构。本文要利用参数对称性来改善收敛速度和泛化性能。","importance":"方法级贡献，对 pretrain 优化有潜在启示。如果参数对称性能被系统利用，可能改善大模型训练的收敛效率和泛化。目前还是理论+小规模验证阶段，但方向有价值。","technical_approach":"核心思路是在优化过程中显式利用参数对称性（如层间 neuron permutation）来约束或引导梯度更新。具体做法是识别模型的对称群，然后设计对称感知的优化步骤——要么通过投影到对称不变子空间，要么通过对称增强来正则化。与 SAM 等平坦极小值方法不同，这里直接从群论结构出发。","experiments":"在标准视觉和语言任务上验证，模型规模偏小。相比标准 SGD/Adam，收敛速度和测试泛化均有改善。具体提升幅度需参考原文数据。","authors_note":"Rose Yu 来自 UC San Diego，在对称性与深度学习交叉方向有系列工作。Robin Walters 在等变网络方面有代表性贡献。"},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"Neural network parameter spaces have symmetries (e.g., neuron permutations) creating equivalent points on the loss surface, yet standard optimizers ignore this structure entirely. This work exploits parameter symmetries to improve convergence and generalization.","importance":"Method-level contribution to optimization theory. Potentially relevant to large-scale pretraining if symmetry-aware optimization can scale, though current validation is at smaller scale.","technical_approach":"Identifies the symmetry group of the model (e.g., permutation symmetries across layers), then designs optimization steps that are aware of these symmetries—either by projecting gradients onto symmetry-invariant subspaces or by symmetry-based regularization. Prior understanding is largely empirical, lacking derivable conditions from a tractable model.","importance":"This is an optimization-mechanism contribution: it links catapult behavior to learning rate, curvature, and initialization in a simplified but computable setting, offering practical guidance for anticipating loss spikes and avoiding instability. For large-scale pretraining, it supports the view that many stability issues can be reasoned about via local second-order structure, informing LR schedules, warmup, and precision choices.","technical_approach":"They approximate training dynamics with quadratic objectives and analyze gradient descent trajectories to characterize regimes where iterates first amplify error along unstable directions and later enter a fast-descent phase due to effective curvature changes. for Reasoning Tasks","authors":["Kaijie Zhu","Jiaao Chen","Jindong Wang","Neil Zhenqiang Gong","Diyi Yang","Xing Xie"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"","venue":"ICLR","year":2024,"track":"main","award":"spotlight","links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/iclr/ZhuC0GY024","doi":""},"_dblp_key":"conf/iclr/ZhuC0GY024","_ee":"https://openreview.net/forum?id=gjfOL9z5Xr","category_id":"mechanism","relevance_score":6,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇工作要解决的是：静态 benchmark 很难真实衡量大语言模型的推理能力，因为一旦题库被污染、模板被记住或 prompt 被过度适配，分数会高但能力判断失真。过去常见做法是不断换数据集或人工出新题，但更新慢、覆盖窄，而且仍然容易被模型或评测流程过拟合。DyVal 试图把评测从‘固定题库打分’改成‘动态生成并控制难度的推理测试’。","importance":"它的重要性在于评测方法级，而不是模型能力本身的提升。对 pretrain 和 posttrain 研究都很有价值，因为推理训练、合成数据训练和 test-time prompting 都强依赖一个可信的能力测量工具；如果评测本身被污染，就很难判断训练改动是真提升还是只是在 benchmark 上讨巧。论文的核心贡献是把动态性、可扩展性和一定程度的统计控制引入推理评测，这比再发布一个静态数据集更耐用。","technical_approach":"核心方法是构造一个动态评测框架：按任务规则在线或半在线生成问题，再通过约束机制控制题目合法性、难度和答案可验证性。它不是单纯用 LLM 出题，而是强调程序化生成、任务模板、答案检验以及评测时的分布刷新，从而降低训练集泄漏和 benchmark memorization 的影响。和传统静态 benchmark 相比，它牺牲了一部分‘题目人工打磨度’，换来更强的时效性和更难被记住的评测分布。设计有效的原因是，推理任务的关键不只是题面文本，而是隐含的组合结构；只要结构在不断变化，死记硬背就更难奏效。","experiments":"从题目和工作定位可以确定，实验重点应是比较静态评测与动态评测下模型排名、分数稳定性和污染敏感度的差异。关键事实通常包括：多个主流 LLM 在静态 benchmark 上得分接近，但在动态生成的推理题上出现更明显分层；部分模型的静态高分不能迁移到动态测试，说明原有评测存在记忆或模板适配成分。若论文包含消融，最有价值的通常是难度控制、题目生成器质量以及不同任务族下排名相关性分析。由于给定信息没有摘要和具体数字，这里不能可靠写出参数量、题量或精确提升幅度。","authors_note":"从作者名单可见 Diyi Yang、Neil Zhenqiang Gong 参与，但无法仅依据给定内容准确确认全部机构与团队背景。"},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper addresses a measurement problem: static reasoning benchmarks are increasingly unreliable because models can memorize datasets, absorb leaked questions, or overfit to prompt templates without genuinely improving reasoning. The usual fix has been to release new test sets, but that is slow, narrow, and still vulnerable to contamination. DyVal proposes shifting from fixed test collections to dynamically generated reasoning evaluation.","importance":"This is important as an evaluation-method paper, not as a new model. It matters for both pretraining and post-training because any claim about reasoning improvements depends on having a trustworthy metric. If the benchmark is contaminated, one cannot tell whether gains come from better learning or simply better benchmark matching. The main value here is introducing dynamism, refreshability, and better control into reasoning evaluation, which is more durable than publishing yet another static dataset.","technical_approach":"The core idea is a dynamic evaluation framework that generates reasoning problems under explicit task rules, while controlling validity, difficulty, and answer verifiability. This is not just 'use an LLM to make questions'. The useful part is the combination of procedural generation, task templates, answer checking, and distribution refresh at evaluation time. Compared with static benchmarks, the framework gives up some hand-crafted polish in exchange for lower leakage risk and harder-to-memorize test distributions. The design works because reasoning benchmarks are defined less by exact wording than by latent combinatorial structure; if that structure keeps changing, memorization helps less.","experiments":"Given the paper’s framing, the main experiments are likely to compare model rankings, score stability, and contamination sensitivity under static versus dynamic evaluation. pruning run can get stuck in a poor sparse solution. Model soups have worked well for dense fine-tuned models because averaging can smooth the loss landscape, but that idea does not transfer directly to sparse models with different masks and connectivity. Sparse Model Soups asks whether model averaging can improve pruning rather than only dense ensembling.","importance":"This is a practical compression-method contribution rather than a pretraining advance. Its value lies in combining two mature ideas, pruning and model averaging, into a recipe that may improve sparse model quality with relatively low conceptual complexity. The broader lesson is that different sparse solutions may inhabit nearby low-loss regions, and averaging can reduce path-specific pruning noise. The limitation is compatibility: the method is most plausible when sparse models are structurally similar enough for averaging to remain meaningful.","technical_approach":"The core method is to average multiple sparse models, either obtained after pruning or along the pruning trajectory, to form a sparse model soup. The main technical difficulty relative to dense soups is mask mismatch. A viable implementation therefore needs to handle support alignment, average only over compatible parameters, or reparameterize before averaging. The intuition is straightforward: any single sparse model may be suboptimal because pruning and retraining are noisy, while averaging compatible sparse solutions can smooth errors and improve robustness. This differs from lottery-ticket-style approaches, which focus on finding one good mask rather than exploiting complementarity across several sparse solutions.","experiments":"A paper like this would normally compare standard pruning methods, retrained pruned models, dense model soups, and the proposed sparse soup under matched sparsity levels. but it is slow on long sequences and suffers from error accumulation. Earlier non-autoregressive methods often needed multiple stages, multiple networks, or paid a large quality penalty, so they were not compelling replacements. This work asks whether a single non-autoregressive Transformer can generate audio well through masked generation.","importance":"This is directly relevant to audio language modeling and to alternative sequence modeling paradigms beyond next-token AR. The significance is not just faster decoding. The broader point is that masked or iterative generation can be competitive for long, high-rate discrete sequences such as audio, where full causal factorization may be unnecessarily expensive. For pretraining research, that is a useful signal: with the right discretization and denoising schedule, single-model non-AR generation may offer a better quality-speed trade-off than standard AR baselines. The boundary is that these methods still rely on iterative refinement, and schedule design matters a lot.","technical_approach":"The core method is masked audio generation with a single non-autoregressive Transformer. The likely recipe is close to MaskGIT-style or masked discrete diffusion generation: start from a sequence with masked positions, iteratively fill tokens, and revisit uncertain positions until the audio sequence is complete. The notable design choice is using one model for the whole generation process instead of a hierarchy of separate models. Important details likely include the masking schedule, remasking ratio, confidence estimation, and support for multi-codebook or hierarchical audio tokens. This works because discrete audio sequences contain substantial local redundancy, so they do not always need fully sequential token-by-token decoding.","experiments":"The key experiments should compare against autoregressive audio models and other non-autoregressive baselines on sample quality and generation speed. Many prior acceleration works focus on one layer, such as attention kernels, communication strategies, or operator fusion, but do not integrate them into a coherent training stack. This paper proposes a multi-level acceleration framework.","importance":"This is a systems-level contribution with direct relevance to pretraining engineering. In large-scale training, wall-clock efficiency is rarely determined by one elegant trick; it depends on whether compute, memory, communication, and scheduling are optimized together. The value of a framework like this, if supported well, is not novelty at any single layer but the demonstration that cross-layer co-design yields more stable and transferable speedups than point solutions. The limitation is that such systems often depend on specific hardware and software assumptions.","technical_approach":"From the title, the core idea is a layered acceleration framework spanning multiple parts of the training stack. A plausible design would combine compute-reduction techniques, kernel-level optimization or fusion, communication-aware parallel scheduling, and memory-saving strategies such as recomputation or chunking. The distinguishing feature versus single-technique papers is not an extreme optimization at one layer, but the coordinated composition of several compatible ones. That is exactly what makes sense in practice: Transformer training is usually constrained by several bottlenecks at once, so reducing FLOPs alone often does not translate into proportional wall-clock speedup.","experiments":"For this kind of work, the important evidence should be end-to-end throughput, time-to-target-loss or time-to-target-accuracy, memory footprint, and scaling behavior, rather than only kernel microbenchmarks.
